Context (read first — assume no prior knowledge of this repo)\n\n`suenot/vasya` is a cross-platform Telegram client (Tauri 2 + React + Rust). The Telegram engine and the agent-facing API live under `app/src-tauri/`:\n\n- `vasya-core/` — Tauri-free grammers (Telegram MTProto) engine, shared by the desktop app and the server.\n- `vasya-server/` — axum API gateway: REST under `/api/v1`, GraphQL at `/api/v1/graphql`, SSE at `/api/v1/events`, OpenAPI at `/api/v1/openapi.json`. This is the **agent-native layer**: scoped `vk_…` API keys, audit log, idempotency, per-user isolation. Library + thin bin.\n- `vasya-mcp/` — TypeScript stdio MCP server that wraps the REST API as tools (config: `VASYA_API_URL`, `VASYA_AGENT_KEY`).\n- `app/src-tauri/src/commands/*.rs` — the Tauri desktop commands = the **full client capability**. Use these as the reference implementation when porting features to the server.\n\nConventions:\n- REST and GraphQL share one implementation via `pub(crate) *_op()` functions in `vasya-server/src/routes/*`.\n- Auth middleware: `vasya-server/src/auth.rs` (`require_auth`) + scope/quota policy in `vasya-server/src/policy.rs` (`agent_policy`, `required_scope`). Scopes live in `vasya-server/src/agent_keys.rs` (`ALL_SCOPES`).\n- axum is **0.8** in vasya-server (path params: `{param}`).\n- Verify: `cargo check` / `cargo test` from `app/src-tauri`; `tsc --noEmit` for `vasya-mcp`/frontend. Keep existing tests green and REST/GraphQL DTOs byte-identical unless the issue says otherwise.\n\n---\n\n## Problem\n\n`vasya-mcp` (`app/src-tauri/vasya-mcp/src/index.ts`) currently exposes **13 tools**, a read-heavy subset of the REST API:\n\n`list_accounts, list_chats, get_contacts, get_messages, send_message, forward_messages, mark_messages_read, search_messages, search_all_messages, global_search, get_forum_topics, list_folders, start_loading_chats`\n\nThe REST API (`vasya-server/src/routes/mod.rs`, ~44 routes) supports much more. An LLM driving the client over MCP therefore **cannot**: run the login flow, delete/log out an account, create a group/channel, delete a chat, write folders, manage tabs, send or download **media**, or fetch avatars/chat photos. Goal: bring MCP to parity with the REST capabilities so an agent can fully operate the messenger.\n\n## What to add (each = one MCP tool mirroring a REST route)\n\nMap every tool to its existing REST endpoint (see `vasya-server/src/routes/mod.rs` for exact paths and `vasya-server/src/routes/*.rs` for request/response DTOs). Add:\n\n- **Auth/login**: `request_login_code` (POST `/telegram/login/code`), `verify_login_code` (POST `/telegram/login/verify`), `submit_2fa_password` (POST `/telegram/login/password`).\n- **Accounts**: `delete_account` (DELETE `/accounts/{acc}`), `get_account_avatar` (GET `/accounts/{acc}/avatar`).\n- **Chats**: `create_group` (POST `/accounts/{acc}/groups`), `create_channel` (POST `/accounts/{acc}/channels`), `delete_chat` (DELETE `/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}`), `load_more_chats` (POST `/accounts/{acc}/chats/load`).\n- **Chat photos**: `get_chat_photo` (GET `…/chats/{chat_id}/photo`), `get_user_photos` (GET `…/chats/{chat_id}/photos`).\n- **Folders (write)**: `save_folder` (POST `/accounts/{acc}/folders`), `delete_folder` (DELETE `/accounts/{acc}/folders/{folder_id}`).\n- **Tabs**: `get_tabs` (GET `/accounts/{acc}/tabs`), `save_tabs` (PUT `/accounts/{acc}/tabs`).\n- **Media**:\n  - `send_media` — upload a file to a chat. The REST contract is **raw body + `x-*` headers** (see `vasya-server/src/routes/messages.rs` `send_media` / `serve_file`): the request body is the raw file bytes, metadata (fileName, caption, etc.) goes in percent-encoded `x-*` headers, `Content-Type: application/octet-stream`. The MCP tool should accept a local file path (read bytes) or base64, plus metadata.\n  - `download_media` — download a message's media (GET `…/chats/{chat_id}/messages/{message_id}/media`); return a saved file path or base64.\n- **Search/read** already covered.\n\n## How to implement\n\n1. In `vasya-mcp/src/index.ts`, add a `server.tool(name, description, zodSchema, handler)` per item, reusing the existing HTTP helper that injects `Authorization: Bearer ${VASYA_AGENT_KEY}` and `VASYA_API_URL`. Follow the structure of the existing 13 tools exactly (don't change their contracts).\n2. For media, implement a small helper to POST raw bytes with `x-*` headers (mirror `app/src/transport/http.ts` `sendMedia` on the frontend for the exact header names).\n3. Surface scope errors clearly: the REST API returns `403 Missing scope: <scope>` when the agent key lacks a scope — pass that message back as the tool error so the caller knows which scope to grant.\n4. Update `vasya-mcp/README` (or the package README) and the tool descriptions.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n\n- `tsc --noEmit` clean in `vasya-mcp`.\n- `tools/list` returns the expanded set; each new tool calls the correct REST route.\n- Manual: against a running `vasya-server` (see `vasya-server/README.md` for how to start one with a local token), `tools/call` succeeds for at least `create_group`, `save_folder`, `send_media`, `download_media`.\n- Existing 13 tools unchanged.\n\n## Out of scope\n\nVoice calls, STT/transcription, and storage-mode (those REST endpoints are still `501` — tracked in separate issues). Don't add MCP tools for them yet.\n","closed_by":null,"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/suenot/vasya/issues/1/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/suenot/vasya/issues/1/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null,"pinned_comment":null}

[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use the Read tool to view and analyze it. Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify that the file is a valid image rather than HTML by using a CLI tool such as the 'file' command. When corrupted or non-image files, such as GitHub \"Not Found\" pages saved as `.png`, are read, they can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and crash the AI solver process. When the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download failed, so do not call Read on that file. Instead: (1) when images are from GitHub issues or PRs, such as URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\", retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) when the retry still fails, skip the image and note that it was unavailable.

[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]       2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/suenot/vasya/issues/8/comments --paginate
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]       3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/suenot/vasya/pulls/8/reviews --paginate
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]       Note: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" only returns conversation comments and misses review comments.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you need the latest comments on the issue, use gh api repos/suenot/vasya/issues/1/comments --paginate.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO] 
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO] Solution development and testing.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When issue is solvable, first create a test that reproduces the problem, then implement the fix.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When coding, commit each atomic step that is useful on its own to the pull request branch so interrupted work remains preserved in the pull request.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you test:
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]       start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]       write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you test integrations, use existing framework.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you write or modify tests, consider setting reasonable timeouts at test, suite, and CI job levels so failures surface quickly instead of hanging.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you see repeated test timeout patterns in CI, investigate the root cause rather than increasing timeouts.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When the issue is unclear, write a comment on the issue with questions.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you encounter problems that you cannot solve yourself and need human help, write a comment on the pull request asking for help.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you need human help, use gh pr comment 8 --body \"your message\" to comment on existing PR.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO] 
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO] Reproducible testing.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When fixing a bug, create a test that reproduces the problem before implementing the fix. When you cannot reproduce the problem, you cannot verify the fix.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When encountering logic bugs, write an automated test that fails due to the bug, then implement the fix to make it pass.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When encountering UI bugs, capture a screenshot showing the problem state, then create a visual regression test or manual verification screenshot after the fix.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When creating tests, prefer minimum reproducible examples, meaning the simplest test case that demonstrates the issue.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When submitting a fix, include in the PR description: (1) how to reproduce the issue, (2) the automated test that verifies the fix, (3) before/after screenshots for UI issues.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When a bug fix does not have a reproducing test, treat the fix as incomplete because regressions can occur later without notice.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO] 
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO] Preparing pull request.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you code, follow contributing guidelines.

[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]       follow style from merged prs for code, title, and description,

[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]       check for latest comments on the issue and pull request to ensure no recent feedback was missed,
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]       double-check that all changes in the pull request address the original requirements of the issue,
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]       check for newly introduced bugs in the pull request by carefully reading gh pr diff,
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]       check that no previously existing features were removed without an explicit request in the issue description, issue comments, or pull request comments.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you finish implementation, use gh pr ready 8.

[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO] GitHub CLI command patterns.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When fetching lists from GitHub API, use the --paginate flag to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/\${owner}/\${repo}/pulls/\${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO] 
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO] Playwright MCP usage (browser automation via mcp__playwright__* tools).
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you develop frontend web applications (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, Angular, etc.), use Playwright MCP tools to test the UI in a real browser.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When WebFetch tool fails to retrieve expected content (e.g., returns empty content, JavaScript-rendered pages, or login-protected pages), use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for web browsing.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When WebSearch tool fails or returns insufficient results, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for internet search.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you need to interact with dynamic web pages that require JavaScript execution, use Playwright MCP tools.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you need to visually verify how a web page looks or take screenshots, use browser_take_screenshot from Playwright MCP.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you need to fill forms, click buttons, or perform user interactions on web pages, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form).
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you need to test responsive design or different viewport sizes, use browser_resize from Playwright MCP.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you finish using the browser, close it with browser_close to free resources.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When reproducing UI bugs, use browser_take_screenshot to capture the problem state before implementing any fix.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When fixing UI bugs, take before/after screenshots to provide visual evidence of the fix for human verification.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When creating UI tests, save baseline screenshots to the repository for visual regression testing.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When verifying UI fixes, compare screenshots to ensure the fix does not introduce unintended visual changes.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO] 
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO] Visual UI work and screenshots.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you work on visual UI changes (frontend, CSS, HTML, design), include a render or screenshot of the final result in the pull request description.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you need to show visual results, take a screenshot and save it to the repository (e.g., in a docs/screenshots/ or assets/ folder).
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When you save screenshots to the repository, use permanent links in the pull request description markdown (e.g., https://github.com/konard/suenot-vasya/blob/issue-1-d5192f2e83f0/docs/screenshots/result.png?raw=true).
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When uploading images, commit them to the branch first, then reference them using the GitHub blob URL format with ?raw=true suffix (works for both public and private repositories).
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When the visual result is important for review, mention it explicitly in the pull request description with the embedded image.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When fixing UI bugs, capture both the \"before\" (problem) and \"after\" (fixed) screenshots as evidence for human verification.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When reporting UI bugs, include a screenshot of the problem state to enable visual verification of the fix.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When the fix is visual, include side-by-side or sequential comparison of before/after states in the PR description.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.003Z] [INFO]    - When possible, create automated visual regression tests to prevent the UI bug from recurring." | jq -c .)

[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO] General guidelines.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]    - When you execute commands and the output becomes large, save the logs to files for easier review.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]    - When running commands, avoid setting a timeout yourself. Let them run as long as needed. The default timeout of 2 minutes is usually enough, and once commands finish, review the logs in the file.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]    - When running sudo commands, especially package installations like apt-get, yum, or npm install, run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]    - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]       Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo suenot/vasya --branch issue-1-d5192f2e83f0 --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]       Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]       Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo suenot/vasya --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]       Step 4: Read each downloaded log file with the Read tool to understand the actual failures
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]       Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]       This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]       Note: If user says "failing" but tools show "passing", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]    - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]    - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]    - When you create debug, test, or example scripts while fixing an issue, keep them in ./examples and/or ./experiments so you can reuse them later.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]    - When you test assumptions, keep experiment scripts in ./experiments.

[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]    - When you read the issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
[2026-06-13T13:12:52.004Z] [INFO]    - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use the Read tool to view and analyze it. Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify that the file is a valid image rather than HTML by using a CLI tool such as the 'file' command. When corrupted or non-image files, such as GitHub "Not Found" pages saved as `.png`, are read, they can cause "Could not process image" errors and crash the AI solver process. When the file command shows "HTML", "text", or "ASCII text", the download failed, so do not call Read on that file. Instead: (1) when images are from GitHub issues or PRs, such as URLs containing "github.com/user-attachments", retry with: curl -L -H "Authorization: token $(gh auth token)" -o <filename> "<url>" (2) when the retry still fails, skip the image and note that it was unavailable.

[2026-06-13T13:12:57.837Z] [INFO]         "type": "tool_result",
[2026-06-13T13:12:57.837Z] [INFO]         "content": "title:\tvasya-mcp: expand MCP tools to full REST coverage (media, folders, tabs, account/chat management, login)\nstate:\tOPEN\nauthor:\tsuenot (Eugen Soloviov)\nlabels:\tenhancement\ncomments:\t0\nassignees:\t\nprojects:\t\nmilestone:\t\nissue-type:\t\nparent:\t\nsub-issues:\t\nsub-issues-completed:\t\nblocked-by:\t\nblocking:\t\nnumber:\t1\n--\n## Context (read first — assume no prior knowledge of this repo)\n\n`suenot/vasya` is a cross-platform Telegram client (Tauri 2 + React + Rust). The Telegram engine and the agent-facing API live under `app/src-tauri/`:\n\n- `vasya-core/` — Tauri-free grammers (Telegram MTProto) engine, shared by the desktop app and the server.\n- `vasya-server/` — axum API gateway: REST under `/api/v1`, GraphQL at `/api/v1/graphql`, SSE at `/api/v1/events`, OpenAPI at `/api/v1/openapi.json`. This is the **agent-native layer**: scoped `vk_…` API keys, audit log, idempotency, per-user isolation. Library + thin bin.\n- `vasya-mcp/` — TypeScript stdio MCP server that wraps the REST API as tools (config: `VASYA_API_URL`, `VASYA_AGENT_KEY`).\n- `app/src-tauri/src/commands/*.rs` — the Tauri desktop commands = the **full client capability**. Use these as the reference implementation when porting features to the server.\n\nConventions:\n- REST and GraphQL share one implementation via `pub(crate) *_op()` functions in `vasya-server/src/routes/*`.\n- Auth middleware: `vasya-server/src/auth.rs` (`require_auth`) + scope/quota policy in `vasya-server/src/policy.rs` (`agent_policy`, `required_scope`). Scopes live in `vasya-server/src/agent_keys.rs` (`ALL_SCOPES`).\n- axum is **0.8** in vasya-server (path params: `{param}`).\n- Verify: `cargo check` / `cargo test` from `app/src-tauri`; `tsc --noEmit` for `vasya-mcp`/frontend. Keep existing tests green and REST/GraphQL DTOs byte-identical unless the issue says otherwise.\n\n---\n\n## Problem\n\n`vasya-mcp` (`app/src-tauri/vasya-mcp/src/index.ts`) currently exposes **13 tools**, a read-heavy subset of the REST API:\n\n`list_accounts, list_chats, get_contacts, get_messages, send_message, forward_messages, mark_messages_read, search_messages, search_all_messages, global_search, get_forum_topics, list_folders, start_loading_chats`\n\nThe REST API (`vasya-server/src/routes/mod.rs`, ~44 routes) supports much more. An LLM driving the client over MCP therefore **cannot**: run the login flow, delete/log out an account, create a group/channel, delete a chat, write folders, manage tabs, send or download **media**, or fetch avatars/chat photos. Goal: bring MCP to parity with the REST capabilities so an agent can fully operate the messenger.\n\n## What to add (each = one MCP tool mirroring a REST route)\n\nMap every tool to its existing REST endpoint (see `vasya-server/src/routes/mod.rs` for exact paths and `vasya-server/src/routes/*.rs` for request/response DTOs). Add:\n\n- **Auth/login**: `request_login_code` (POST `/telegram/login/code`), `verify_login_code` (POST `/telegram/login/verify`), `submit_2fa_password` (POST `/telegram/login/password`).\n- **Accounts**: `delete_account` (DELETE `/accounts/{acc}`), `get_account_avatar` (GET `/accounts/{acc}/avatar`).\n- **Chats**: `create_group` (POST `/accounts/{acc}/groups`), `create_channel` (POST `/accounts/{acc}/channels`), `delete_chat` (DELETE `/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}`), `load_more_chats` (POST `/accounts/{acc}/chats/load`).\n- **Chat photos**: `get_chat_photo` (GET `…/chats/{chat_id}/photo`), `get_user_photos` (GET `…/chats/{chat_id}/photos`).\n- **Folders (write)**: `save_folder` (POST `/accounts/{acc}/folders`), `delete_folder` (DELETE `/accounts/{acc}/folders/{folder_id}`).\n- **Tabs**: `get_tabs` (GET `/accounts/{acc}/tabs`), `save_tabs` (PUT `/accounts/{acc}/tabs`).\n- **Media**:\n  - `send_media` — upload a file to a chat. The REST contract is **raw body + `x-*` headers** (see `vasya-server/src/routes/messages.rs` `send_media` / `serve_file`): the request body is the raw file bytes, metadata (fileName, caption, etc.) goes in percent-encoded `x-*` headers, `Content-Type: application/octet-stream`. The MCP tool should accept a local file path (read bytes) or base64, plus metadata.\n  - `download_media` — download a message's media (GET `…/chats/{chat_id}/messages/{message_id}/media`); return a saved file path or base64.\n- **Search/read** already covered.\n\n## How to implement\n\n1. In `vasya-mcp/src/index.ts`, add a `server.tool(name, description, zodSchema, handler)` per item, reusing the existing HTTP helper that injects `Authorization: Bearer ${VASYA_AGENT_KEY}` and `VASYA_API_URL`. Follow the structure of the existing 13 tools exactly (don't change their contracts).\n2. For media, implement a small helper to POST raw bytes with `x-*` headers (mirror `app/src/transport/http.ts` `sendMedia` on the frontend for the exact header names).\n3. Surface scope errors clearly: the REST API returns `403 Missing scope: <scope>` when the agent key lacks a scope — pass that message back as the tool error so the caller knows which scope to grant.\n4. Update `vasya-mcp/README` (or the package README) and the tool descriptions.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n\n- `tsc --noEmit` clean in `vasya-mcp`.\n- `tools/list` returns the expanded set; each new tool calls the correct REST route.\n- Manual: against a running `vasya-server` (see `vasya-server/README.md` for how to start one with a local token), `tools/call` succeeds for at least `create_group`, `save_folder`, `send_media`, `download_media`.\n- Existing 13 tools unchanged.\n\n## Out of scope\n\nVoice calls, STT/transcription, and storage-mode (those REST endpoints are still `501` — tracked in separate issues). Don't add MCP tools for them yet.",

Configure with:\n *   VASYA_API_URL   base URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8787)\n *   VASYA_AGENT_KEY scoped agent key (vk_...) or any bearer the API accepts\n *\n * Tool availability follows the key's scopes — out-of-scope calls return\n * the API's 403 with the missing scope named, so agents can self-diagnose.\n */\n\nimport { McpServer } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js\";\nimport { StdioServerTransport } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js\";\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\nimport { randomUUID } from \"node:crypto\";\n\nconst BASE_URL = (process.env.VASYA_API_URL ?? \"http://127.0.0.1:8787\").replace(/\\/$/, \"\");\nconst AGENT_KEY = process.env.VASYA_AGENT_KEY;\nif (!AGENT_KEY) {\n  console.error(\"VASYA_AGENT_KEY is required (create one via POST /api/v1/agent-keys)\");\n  process.exit(1);\n}\n\nasync function api(\n  method: string,\n  path: string,\n  body?: unknown,\n  idempotent = false,\n): Promise<string> {\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = { Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}` };\n  if (body !== undefined) headers[\"content-type\"] = \"application/json\";\n  // Safe retries for mutations: the server replays on duplicate keys.\n  if (idempotent) headers[\"Idempotency-Key\"] = randomUUID();\n\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    method,\n    headers,\n    body: body === undefined ? undefined : JSON.stringify(body),\n  });\n\n  const text = await response.text();\n  if (!response.ok) {\n    const retryAfter = response.headers.get(\"retry-after\");\n    throw new Error(\n      `${method} ${path} -> ${response.status}${retryAfter ? ` (retry after ${retryAfter}s)` : \"\"}: ${text}`,\n    );\n  }\n  return text.length > 0 ? text : JSON.stringify({ ok: true });\n}\n\nconst asText = (text: string) => ({ content: [{ type: \"text\" as const, text }] });\n\nconst server = new McpServer({ name: \"vasya-mcp\", version: \"0.1.0\" });\n\nconst accountId = z.string().describe(\"Telegram account id (from list_accounts)\");\nconst chatId = z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Chat id (bot-api format, from list_chats)\");\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_accounts\",\n  \"List the connected Telegram accounts (id, phone, connected). Account ids are required by every other tool.\",\n  {},\n  async () => asText(await api(\"GET\", \"/accounts\")),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_chats\",\n  \"List an account's chats (id, title, unread count, type user/group/channel, last message preview). Use offsetId for pagination (pass the lowest message id from the previous page). topicId reads one forum topic.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Max messages (default 50)\"),\n    offsetId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Only messages older than this id\"),\n    topicId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Forum topic id (from get_forum_topics)\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, limit, offsetId, topicId }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    if (offsetId !== undefined) params.set(\"offset_id\", String(offsetId));\n    if (topicId !== undefined) params.set(\"topic_id\", String(topicId));\n    const qs = params.size > 0 ? `?${params}` : \"\";\n    return asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages${qs}`));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"send_message\",\n  \"Send a text message to a chat (optionally into a forum topic). Returns the sent message. [2026-06-13T13:13:23.508Z] [INFO]       "content": "//! Route modules and router assembly.\n//!\n//! Pathing convention (plan §4.2): /api/v1, accounts scoped as\n//! /accounts/{acc}/..., raw bodies for media upload, bytes out for media\n//! download. Calls, group calls, STT and storage-mode are 501 this phase.\n\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit;\nuse axum::routing::{delete, get, post, put};\nuse axum::{middleware, Router};\n\nuse crate::auth::UserId;\nuse crate::context::ServerContext;\nuse crate::error::ApiError;\n\npub mod accounts;\npub mod agent_keys;\npub mod chats;\npub mod events;\npub mod folders;\npub mod graphql_http;\npub mod media;\npub mod messages;\npub mod search;\npub mod stubs;\npub mod telegram_auth;\npub mod topics;\n\n/// Max raw media upload size.\nconst MEDIA_BODY_LIMIT: usize = 128 * 1024 * 1024;\n\n/// Ownership check + client lookup used by every account-scoped handler.\npub(crate) async fn account_client(\n    ctx: &ServerContext,\n    user: &UserId,\n    account_id: &str,\n) -> Result<\n    std::sync::Arc<vasya_core::telegram::client_manager::TelegramClientWrapper>,\n    ApiError,\n> {\n    ctx.accounts.ensure_access(&user.0, account_id)?;\n    ctx.manager\n        .get_client(account_id)\n        .await\n        .ok_or_else(|| ApiError::NotFound(\"Client not found for this account\".into()))\n}\n\npub fn api_router(ctx: Arc<ServerContext>) -> Router {\n    let schema = crate::graphql::build_schema(ctx.clone());\n\n    let protected = Router::new()\n        // GraphQL queries/mutations (same bearer middleware as REST)\n        .route(\"/graphql\", post(graphql_http::graphql_post))\n        // Telegram API credentials\n        .route(\"/telegram/credentials\", get(telegram_auth::credentials_status))\n        .route(\"/telegram/credentials\", put(telegram_auth::update_credentials))\n        // Telegram login flow\n        .route(\"/telegram/login/code\", post(telegram_auth::request_login_code))\n        .route(\"/telegram/login/verify\", post(telegram_auth::verify_code))\n        .route(\"/telegram/login/password\", post(telegram_auth::check_password))\n        // Accounts\n        .route(\"/accounts\", get(accounts::list_accounts))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}\", delete(accounts::logout))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/avatar\", get(accounts::my_avatar))\n        // Chats\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/chats\", get(chats::list_chats))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/chats/load\", post(chats::start_loading_chats))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}\", delete(chats::delete_and_leave_chat))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/groups\", post(chats::create_group))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/channels\", post(chats::create_channel))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/contacts\", get(chats::get_contacts))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/photo\", get(media::chat_photo))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/photos\", get(media::user_photos))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/photos/{index}\", get(media::user_photo_by_index))\n        // Messages\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/messages\", get(messages::get_messages))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/messages\", post(messages::send_message))\n        .route(\n            \"/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/media\",\n            post(messages::send_media).layer(DefaultBodyLimit::max(MEDIA_BODY_LIMIT)),\n        )\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/messages/forward\", post(messages::forward_messages))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/read\", post(messages::mark_messages_read))\n        .route(\n            \"/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/messages/{message_id}/media\",\n            get(media::download_media),\n        )\n        // Search\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/messages/search\", get(messages::search_messages))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/search\", get(search::global_search))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/messages/search\", get(search::search_all_messages))\n        // Topics\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/topics\", get(topics::get_forum_topics))\n        // Folders / tabs\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/folders\", get(folders::get_folders))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/folders\", post(folders::save_folder))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/folders/{folder_id}\", delete(folders::delete_folder))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/tabs\", get(folders::get_tabs))\n        .route(\"/accounts/{acc}/tabs\", put(folders::save_tabs))\n        // Realtime bus as SSE (same bus feeds the GraphQL subscriptions)\n        .route(\"/events\", get(events::sse_events))\n        // Agent key management + audit (human sessions only — the agent\n        // policy middleware rejects agent keys here)\n        .route(\"/agent-keys\", post(agent_keys::create_key))\n        .route(\"/agent-keys\", get(agent_keys::list_keys))\n        .route(\"/agent-keys/scopes\", get(agent_keys::list_scopes))\n        .route(\"/agent-keys/{key_id}\", delete(agent_keys::revoke_key))\n        .route(\"/audit\", get(agent_keys::read_audit))\n        // 501 stubs: calls, group calls, STT, storage-mode\n        .merge(stubs::router())\n        // Layer order (inner→outer as added): idempotency → agent policy →\n        // audit → auth. Audit records policy rejections and replays.\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn_with_state(ctx.clone(), crate::policy::idempotency))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn_with_state(ctx.clone(), crate::policy::agent_policy))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn_with_state(ctx.clone(), crate::policy::audit_mutations))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn_with_state(ctx.clone(), crate::auth::require_auth));\n\n    let mut public = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/health\", get(health))\n        .route(\"/openapi.json\", get(crate::openapi::openapi_json))\n        // Machine-readable contract, public like /openapi.json\n        .route(\"/graphql/sdl\", get(graphql_http::graphql_sdl))\n        // Subscriptions: auth happens on connection_init, not headers\n        .route(\"/graphql/ws\", get(graphql_http::graphql_ws));\n\n    if ctx.graphql_playground {\n        public = public.route(\"/graphql/playground\", get(graphql_http::graphql_playground));\n    }\n\n    Router::new()\n        .nest(\"/api/v1\", public.merge(protected))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(schema))\n        .with_state(ctx)\n}\n\nasync fn health() -> axum::Json<serde_json::Value> {\n    axum::Json(serde_json::json!({ \"status\": \"ok\" }))\n}\n",

  .remove(&account_id)\n162\t        .ok_or_else(|| ApiError::BadRequest(\"Login session expired or invalid\".into()))?;\n163\t\n164\t    let wrapper = ctx\n165\t        .manager\n166\t dialog_to_chat(dialog: &grammers_client::types::Dialog) -> (i64, Chat) {\n48\t    let peer = &dialog.peer;\n49\t    let (chat_type, is_forum) = match peer {\n50\t        Peer::User(_) => (\"user\", };\n56\t            (\"group\", forum)\n57\t        }\n58\t        Peer::Channel(c) => (\"channel\", c.raw.forum),\n59\t    };\n60\t\n61\t    let title = }\n120\t\n121\t        if emit_progress {\n122\t            if let Ok(mut payload) = serde_json::to_value(&chat) {\n123\t                payload[\"accountId\"] = }\n130\t\n131\t    ctx.chat_cache\n132\t        .write()\n133\t        .await\n134\t        .insert(account_id.to_string(), chats.clone());\n135\t\n136\t [2026-06-13T13:13:27.555Z] [INFO]       "content": "//! Chat operations (parity with commands/chats.rs).\n//!\n//! Differences from the desktop commands, by design:\n//! * `avatar_path` stays `None` — avatars are served on demand by the\n//!   /photo endpoint instead of being paths on someone else's disk.\n//! * The chat cache is in server memory (replaced per listing) instead of\n//!   the app's local SQLite.\n//! * Progressive loading emits `chat-loaded` / `chats-loading-complete`\n//!   into the event bus with an extra `accountId` field (the bus carries\n//!   all accounts; desktop events are implicitly single-app).\n\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse axum::extract::{Path, Query, State};\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::{Extension, Json};\nuse grammers_client::types::Peer;\nuse grammers_session::defs::PeerRef;\nuse grammers_tl_types as tl;\nuse serde::Deserialize;\nuse vasya_core::events::EventSink;\nuse vasya_core::telegram::client_manager::TelegramClientWrapper;\n\nuse crate::auth::UserId;\nuse crate::context::ServerContext;\nuse crate::dto::Chat;\nuse crate::error::ApiError;\nuse crate::peer::resolve_peer;\nuse crate::routes::account_client;\n\n/// Extract unread_count and is_muted from the raw TL Dialog enum.\nfn extract_dialog_meta(raw: &tl::enums::Dialog) -> (i32, bool) {\n    match raw {\n        tl::enums::Dialog::Dialog(d) => {\n            let muted = match &d.notify_settings {\n                tl::enums::PeerNotifySettings::Settings(s) => {\n                    // mute_until > 0 means muted (i32::MAX = \"forever\")\n                    s.mute_until.map_or(false, |t| t > 0)\n                }\n            };\n            (d.unread_count, muted)\n        }\n        tl::enums::Dialog::Folder(_) => (0, false),\n    }\n}\n\nfn dialog_to_chat(dialog: &grammers_client::types::Dialog) -> (i64, Chat) {\n    let peer = &dialog.peer;\n    let (chat_type, is_forum) = match peer {\n        Peer::User(_) => (\"user\", false),\n        Peer::Group(g) => {\n            let forum = match &g.raw {\n                tl::enums::Chat::Channel(ch) => ch.forum,\n                _ => false,\n            };\n            (\"group\", forum)\n        }\n        Peer::Channel(c) => (\"channel\", c.raw.forum),\n    };\n\n    let title = peer.name().unwrap_or(\"Unknown\").to_string();\n    let username = match peer {\n        Peer::User(u) => u.username().map(|s| s.to_string()),\n        Peer::Channel(c) => c.username().map(|s| s.to_string()),\n        Peer::Group(g) => g.username().map(|s| s.to_string()),\n    };\n\n    let chat_id = PeerRef::from(peer).id.bot_api_dialog_id();\n\n    let last_message = dialog.last_message.as_ref().map(|msg| {\n        let text = msg.text();\n        if text.chars().count() > 100 {\n            let truncated: String = text.chars().take(100).collect();\n            format!(\"{}...\", truncated)\n        } else {\n            text.to_string()\n        }\n    });\n\n    let (unread_count, is_muted) = extract_dialog_meta(&dialog.raw);\n\n    (\n        chat_id,\n        Chat {\n            id: chat_id,\n            title,\n            username,\n            unread_count,\n            chat_type: chat_type.to_string(),\n            last_message,\n            avatar_path: None,\n            is_forum,\n            is_muted,\n        },\n    )\n}\n\n/// Iterate all dialogs, fill the wrapper's peer cache, return chats in\n/// dialog order. Optionally emits a `chat-loaded` event per chat.\nasync fn collect_chats(\n    ctx: &ServerContext,\n    wrapper: &TelegramClientWrapper,\n    account_id: &str,\n    emit_progress: bool,\n) -> Result<Vec<Chat>, ApiError> {\n    let mut dialogs = wrapper.client.iter_dialogs();\n    let mut chats = Vec::new();\n\n    while let Some(dialog) = dialogs\n        .next()\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ApiError::telegram(format!(\"Failed to get dialogs: {e}\")))?\n    {\n        let (chat_id, chat) = dialog_to_chat(&dialog);\n\n        {\n            let mut peers = wrapper.peers.write().await;\n            peers.insert(chat_id, dialog.peer.clone());\n        }\n\n        if emit_progress {\n            if let Ok(mut payload) = serde_json::to_value(&chat) {\n                payload[\"accountId\"] = serde_json::Value::String(account_id.to_string());\n                ctx.events.emit(\"chat-loaded\", payload);\n            }\n        }\n\n        chats.push(chat);\n    }\n\n    ctx.chat_cache\n        .write()\n        .await\n        .insert(account_id.to_string(), chats.clone());\n\n    Ok(chats)\n}\n\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\npub struct ListChatsQuery {\n    /// \"cache\" (default; like get_cached_chats) or \"live\" (like get_chats —\n    /// fresh dialog iteration).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub source: Option<String>,\n}\n\npub(crate) async fn list_chats_op(\n    ctx: &Arc<ServerContext>,\n    user: &UserId,\n    account_id: &str,\n    live: bool,\n) -> Result<Vec<Chat>, ApiError> {\n    let wrapper = account_client(ctx, user, account_id).await?;\n\n    if !live {\n        if let Some(cached) = ctx.chat_cache.read().await.get(account_id) {\n            return Ok(cached.clone());\n        }\n    }\n\n    collect_chats(ctx, &wrapper, account_id, false).await\n}\n\npub async fn list_chats(\n    State(ctx): State<Arc<ServerContext>>,\n    user: Extension<UserId>,\n    Path(account_id): Path<String>,\n    Query(query): Query<ListChatsQuery>,\n) -> Result<Json<Vec<Chat>>, ApiError> {\n    let live = matches!(query.source.as_deref(), Some(\"live\"));\n    Ok(Json(list_chats_op(&ctx, &user.0, &account_id, live).await?))\n}\n\n/// Kick off progressive loading: results stream as `chat-loaded` events on\n/// the bus and land in the chat cache.\npub(crate) async fn start_loading_chats_op(\n    ctx: &Arc<ServerContext>,\n    user: &UserId,\n    account_id: &str,\n) -> Result<(), ApiError> {\n    let wrapper = account_client(ctx, user, account_id).await?;\n\n    let ctx = ctx.clone();\n    let account_id = account_id.to_string();\n    tokio::spawn(async move {\n        match collect_chats(&ctx, &wrapper, &account_id, true).await {\n            Ok(chats) => {\n                ctx.events.emit(\n                    \"chats-loading-complete\",\n                    serde_json::json!({ \"accountId\": account_id, \"count\": chats.len() }),\n                );\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::error!(account_id = %account_id, error = %e, \"Chat loading failed\");\n            }\n        }\n    });\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\npub async fn start_loading_chats(\n    State(ctx): State<Arc<ServerContext>>,\n    user: Extension<UserId>,\n    Path(account_id): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<StatusCode, ApiError> {\n    start_loading_chats_op(&ctx, &user.0, &account_id).await?;\n    Ok(StatusCode::ACCEPTED)\n}\n\npub(crate) async fn delete_and_leave_chat_op(\n    ctx: &Arc<ServerContext>,\n    user: &UserId,\n    account_id: &str,\n    chat_id: i64,\n) -> Result<(), ApiError> {\n    tracing::info!(account_id = %account_id, chat_id, \"Delete and leave chat\");\n    let wrapper = account_client(ctx, user, account_id).await?;\n    ctx.rate.check_mutation(account_id)?;\n\n    let peer = resolve_peer(&wrapper, chat_id).await?;\n\n    // delete_dialog handles all peer types: channels/megagroups ->\n    // LeaveChannel, groups -> DeleteChatUser, users -> DeleteHistory\n    wrapper\n        .client\n        .delete_dialog(&peer)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ApiError::telegram(format!(\"Failed to delete and leave chat: {e}\")))?;\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\npub async fn delete_and_leave_chat(\n    State(ctx): State<Arc<ServerContext>>,\n    user: Extension<UserId>,\n    Path((account_id, chat_id)): Path<(String, i64)>,\n) -> Result<StatusCode, ApiError> {\n    delete_and_leave_chat_op(&ctx, &user.0, &account_id, chat_id).await?;\n    Ok(StatusCode::NO_CONTENT)\n}\n\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"camelCase\")]\npub struct CreateGroupRequest {\n    pub title: String,\n    pub user_ids: Vec<i64>,\n}\n\n#[derive(serde::Serialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"camelCase\")]\npub struct CreatedChatResponse {\n    pub chat_id: i64,\n}\n\npub(crate) async fn create_group_op(\n    ctx: &Arc<ServerContext>,\n    user: &UserId,\n    account_id: &str,\n    title: String,\n    user_ids: &[i64],\n) -> Result<i64, ApiError> {\n    tracing::info!(account_id = %account_id, title = %title, \"Creating group\");\n    let wrapper = account_client(ctx, user, account_id).await?;\n    ctx.rate.check_mutation(account_id)?;\n\n    let mut input_users = Vec::new();\n    for uid in user_ids {\n        let peer = resolve_peer(&wrapper, *uid).await?;\n        let peer_ref = PeerRef::from(&peer);\n        let input_user: tl::enums::InputUser = peer_ref.into();\n        input_users.push(input_user);\n    }\n\n    let request = tl::functions::messages::CreateChat {\n        users: input_users,\n        title,\n        ttl_period: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = wrapper\n        .client\n        .invoke(&request)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ApiError::telegram(format!(\"Failed to create group: {e}\")))?;\n\n    let chat_id = match &result {\n        tl::enums::messages::InvitedUsers::Users(invited) => match &invited.updates {\n            tl::enums::Updates::Updates(u) => u\n                .chats\n                .first()\n                .map(|c| match c {\n                    tl::enums::Chat::Chat(chat) => -(chat.id as i64), // Bot API format for groups\n                    tl::enums::Chat::Channel(ch) => {\n                        let id = ch.id as i64;\n                        -1_000_000_000_000 - id // Bot API format for channels/supergroups\n                    }\n                    _ => 0,\n                })\n                .unwrap_or(0),\n            _ => 0,\n        },\n    };\n\n    tracing::info!(account_id = %account_id, chat_id, \"Group created\");\n    Ok(chat_id)\n}\n\npub async fn create_group(\n    State(ctx): State<Arc<ServerContext>>,\n    user: Extension<UserId>,\n    Path(account_id): Path<String>,\n    Json(req): Json<CreateGroupRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<CreatedChatResponse>, ApiError> {\n    let chat_id = create_group_op(&ctx, &user.0, &account_id, req.title, &req.user_ids).await?;\n    Ok(Json(CreatedChatResponse { chat_id }))\n}\n\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"camelCase\")]\npub struct CreateChannelRequest {\n    pub title: String,\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub about: String,\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub is_megagroup: bool,\n}\n\npub(crate) async fn create_channel_op(\n    ctx: &Arc<ServerContext>,\n    user: &UserId,\n    account_id: &str,\n    title: String,\n    about: String,\n    is_megagroup: bool,\n) -> Result<i64, ApiError> {\n    tracing::info!(account_id = %account_id, title = %title, is_megagroup, \"Creating channel\");\n    let wrapper = account_client(ctx, user, account_id).await?;\n    ctx.rate.check_mutation(account_id)?;\n\n    let request = tl::functions::channels::CreateChannel {\n        broadcast: !is_megagroup,\n        megagroup: is_megagroup,\n        for_import: false,\n        forum: false,\n        title,\n        about,\n        geo_point: None,\n        address: None,\n        ttl_period: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = wrapper\n        .client\n        .invoke(&request)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ApiError::telegram(format!(\"Failed to create channel: {e}\")))?;\n\n    let chat_id = match &result {\n        tl::enums::Updates::Updates(u) => u\n            .chats\n            .first()\n            .map(|c| match c {\n                tl::enums::Chat::Channel(ch) => {\n                    let id = ch.id as i64;\n                    -1_000_000_000_000 - id\n                }\n                _ => 0,\n            })\n            .unwrap_or(0),\n        _ => 0,\n    };\n\n    tracing::info!(account_id = %account_id, chat_id, \"Channel created\");\n    Ok(chat_id)\n}\n\npub async fn create_channel(\n    State(ctx): State<Arc<ServerContext>>,\n    user: Extension<UserId>,\n    Path(account_id): Path<String>,\n    Json(req):       .await?;\n    Ok(Json(CreatedChatResponse { chat_id }))\n}\n\n/// Contacts = user-type chats from the cache (parity with get_contacts,\n/// which filters the app's chat DB the same way).\npub(crate) async fn get_contacts_op(\n    ctx: &Arc<ServerContext>,\n    user: &UserId,\n    account_id: &str,\n) -> Result<Vec<Chat>, ApiError> {\n    let wrapper = account_client(ctx, user, account_id).await?;\n\n    let cached = ctx.chat_cache.read().await.get(account_id).cloned();\n    let chats = match cached {\n        Some(chats) => chats,\n        None => collect_chats(ctx, .join(format!(\"{account_id}.{kind}.json\"))\n55\t}\n56\t\n57\tasync fn read_list<T: serde::de::DeserializeOwned>(path: &PathBuf) -> Result<Vec<T>, ApiError> {\n58\t    match        Err(e) => Err(ApiError::internal(e)),\n62\t    }\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\tasync fn write_list<T: serde::Serialize>(path: &PathBuf, items: &[T]) -> Result<(), ApiError> {\n66\t    if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n67\t        tokio::fs::create_dir_all(parent).await.map_err(ApiError::internal)?;\n68\t    }\n69\t    let raw = serde_json::to_vec_pretty(items).map_err(ApiError::internal)?;\n70\t    tokio::fs::write(path, raw).await.map_err(ApiError::internal)\n71\t}\n72\t\n73\tfn check(ctx: &ServerContext, user: &UserId, account_id: &str) -> Result<(), ApiError> {\n74\t [2026-06-13T13:13:31.202Z] [INFO]       "content": "//! Folders and tabs (parity with commands/folders.rs).\n//!\n//! The desktop app persists these in its local storage; here they live in\n//! per-(user, account) JSON files under the data dir so embedded-local mode\n//! needs no database. Validation rules are ported verbatim.\n\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse axum::extract::{Path, State};\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::{Extension, Json};\n\nuse crate::auth::UserId;\nuse crate::context::ServerContext;\nuse crate::dto::{FolderRecord, TabRecord};\nuse crate::error::ApiError;\n\n/// Maximum allowed sort_order value to prevent overflow / abuse\nconst MAX_SORT_ORDER: i32 = 10_000;\n\nfn validate_account_id(account_id: &str) -> Result<(), ApiError> {\n    if account_id.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ApiError::BadRequest(\"account_id must not be empty\".into()));\n    }\n    if account_id.len() > 128 {\n        return Err(ApiError::BadRequest(\"account_id too long\".into()));\n    }\n    if !account_id\n        .chars()\n        .all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '-' || c == '_')\n    {\n        return Err(ApiError::BadRequest(\n            \"account_id contains invalid characters\".into(),\n        ));\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn validate_sort_order(sort_order: i32) -> Result<(), ApiError> {\n    if sort_order < 0 || sort_order > MAX_SORT_ORDER {\n        return Err(ApiError::BadRequest(format!(\n            \"sort_order must be between 0 and {MAX_SORT_ORDER}\"\n        )));\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn store_path(ctx: &ServerContext, user: &str, account_id: &str, kind: &str) -> PathBuf {\n    // user ids are uuids or \"local\"; account ids are validated above.\n    ctx.data_dir\n        .join(\"ui-state\")\n        .join(user)\n        .join(format!(\"{account_id}.{kind}.json\"))\n}\n\nasync fn read_list<T: serde::de::DeserializeOwned>(path: &PathBuf) -> Result<Vec<T>, ApiError> {\n    match tokio::fs::read(path).await {\n        Ok(raw) => serde_json::from_slice(&raw).map_err(ApiError::internal),\n        Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => Ok(Vec::new()),\n        Err(e) => Err(ApiError::internal(e)),\n    }\n}\n\nasync fn write_list<T: serde::Serialize>(path: &PathBuf, items: &[T]) -> Result<(), ApiError> {\n    if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n        tokio::fs::create_dir_all(parent).await.map_err(ApiError::internal)?;\n    }\n    let raw = serde_json::to_vec_pretty(items).map_err(ApiError::internal)?;\n    tokio::fs::write(path, raw).await.map_err(ApiError::internal)\n}\n\nfn check(ctx: &ServerContext, user: &UserId, account_id: &str) -> Result<(), ApiError> {\n    validate_account_id(account_id)?;\n    ctx.accounts.ensure_access(&user.0, account_id)\n}\n\npub(crate) async fn get_folders_op(\n    ctx: &ServerContext,\n    user: &UserId,\n    account_id: &str,\n) -> Result<Vec<FolderRecord>, ApiError> check(ctx, user, account_id)?;\n    validate_sort_order(folder.sort_order)?;\n\n    let path = store_path(ctx, &user.0, account_id, \"folders\");\n    let mut folders: Vec<FolderRecord> = read_list(&path).await?;\n    folders.retain(|f| f.id != folder.id);\n    folders.push(folder);\n    folders.sort_by_key(|f| f.sort_order);\n    write_list(&path, &folders).await\n}\n\npub async fn The desktop\n2\t//! commands return local file paths; over HTTP we stream the bytes and keep\n3\t//! a server-side disk cache.\n4\t\n5\tuse std::path::Path as FsPath;\n6\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n7\t\n8\tuse axum::extract::{Path, State};\n9\tuse axum::http::header;\n10\tuse axum::response::Response;\n11\tuse axum::{Extension, Json};\n12\tuse grammers_client::types::Message as GrammersMessage;\n13\t\n14\tuse crate::auth::UserId;\n15\tuse crate::context::ServerContext;\n16\tuse crate::error::ApiError;\n17\tuse crate::flood::with_flood_wait_retry;\n18\tuse crate::peer::resolve_peer;\n19\tuse crate::routes::account_client;\n20\t\n21\t/// Read a cached file from disk into a bytes response.\n22\t///\n23\t/// We serve user-supplied bytes from the API origin, so the response is\n24\t/// hardened against content-sniffing / stored-XSS: `nosniff` pins the\n25\t/// declared type, a locked-down CSP (`default-src 'none'; sandbox`) prevents\n26\t/// any markup that slips through from executing script in our origin, and\n27\t/// `disposition` (\"inline\" for known-renderable media, \"attachment\" for\n28\t/// everything else) stops the browser treating a download as a page.\n29\tpub(crate) async fn serve_file(\n30\t    path: &FsPath,\n31\t    content_type: &str,\n32\t    disposition: &str,\n33\t) -> Result<Response, ApiError> {\n34\t    let bytes = tokio::fs::read(path).await.map_err(ApiError::internal)?;\n35\t    Response::builder()\n36\t        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, content_type)\n37\t        .header(header::CONTENT_DISPOSITION, disposition)\n38\t        .header(header::X_CONTENT_TYPE_OPTIONS, \"nosniff\")\n39\t        .header(header::CONTENT_SECURITY_POLICY, \"default-src 'none'; sandbox\")\n40\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(bytes))\n41\t        .map_err(ApiError::internal)\n42\t}\n43\t\n44\t/// Map a raw Telegram mime type to a `(content_type, disposition)` pair that\n45\t/// is safe to serve from the API origin. Only image (excluding SVG, which can\n46\t/// script), audio and video render inline; anything else — notably\n47\t/// `text/html`, `image/svg+xml`, `application/xhtml+xml`, `*/*+xml` — is\n48\t/// neutralised to an opaque download.\n49\tfn safe_serving(raw: &str) -> (String, &'static str) {\n50\t    let base = raw.split(';').next().unwrap_or(\"\").trim().to_ascii_lowercase();\n51\t    let inline = (base.starts_with(\"image/\") && base != \"image/svg+xml\")\n52\t        || base.starts_with(\"audio/\")\n53\t        || base.starts_with(\"video/\");\n54\t    if inline {\n55\t        (base, \"inline\")\n56\t    } else {\n57\t        (\"application/octet-stream\".to_string(), \"attachment\")\n58\t    }\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\t/// Get file extension from media type (ported from commands/media.rs).\n62\tfn media_extension(media: &grammers_client::types::Media) -> String {\n63\t    match media {\n64\t        grammers_client::types::Media::Photo(_) => \"jpg\".to_string(),\n65\t        grammers_client::types::Media::Document(doc) => doc\n66\t            .mime_type()\n67\t            .map(|mime| match mime {\n68\t                \"audio/ogg\" | \"audio/opus\" | \"audio/ogg; codecs=opus\" => \"ogg\",\n69\t                m if m.starts_with(\"video/\") => \"mp4\",\n70\t                m if m.starts_with(\"audio/\") => \"mp3\",\n71\t                m if m.starts_with(\"image/\") => m.split('/').nth(1).unwrap_or(\"dat\"),\n72\t                _ => \"dat\",\n73\t            })\n74\t            .unwrap_or(\"dat\")\n75\t            .to_string(),\n76\t        _ => \"dat\".to_string(),\n77\t    }\n78\t}\n79\t\n80\tfn media_content_type(media: &grammers_client::types::Media) -> String {\n81\t    match media {\n82\t        grammers_client::types::Media::Photo(_) => \"image/jpeg\".to_string(),\n83\t        grammers_client::types::Media::Document(doc) => doc\n84\t            .mime_type()\n85\t            .unwrap_or(\"application/octet-stream\")\n86\t            .to_string(),\n87\t        _ => \"application/octet-stream\".to_string(),\n88\t    }\n89\t}\n90\t\n91\t/// Download the media of one message and stream it back.\n92\tpub async fn download_media(\n93\t    State(ctx): State<Arc<ServerContext>>,\n94\t    user: Extension<UserId>,\n95\t    Path((account_id, chat_id, message_id)): Path<(String, i64, i32)>,\n96\t) -> Result<Response, ApiError> {\n97\t    tracing::info!(account_id = %account_id, chat_id, message_id, \"Download media requested\");\n98\t\n99\t    let wrapper = account_client(&ctx, &user.0, &account_id).await?;\n100\t    let chat = resolve_peer(&wrapper, chat_id).await?;\n101\t\n102\t    // Find the specific message (same window as the desktop command)\n103\t    let mut messages_iter = wrapper\n104\t        .client\n105\t        .iter_messages(&chat)\n106\t        .offset_id(message_id + 1)\n107\t        .limit(50);\n108\t\n109\t    let mut message = target_message.ok_or_else(|| {\n122\t        ApiError::NotFound(format!(\"Message {message_id} not found in chat {chat_id}\"))\n123\t    })?;\n124\t\n125\t    let media = message\n126\t        .media()\n127\t        .ok_or_else(|| ApiError::NotFound(\"Message has no media\".into()))?;\n128\t\n129\t    // WebPage previews are not downloadable files\n130\t    if matches!(media, ctx.media_dir.join(format!(\"chat_{}\", chat_id.unsigned_abs()));\n135\t    tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&media_dir)\n136\t        .await\n137\t        .map_err(ApiError::internal)?;\n138\t\n139\t    let extension = media_extension(&media);\n140\t    let timestamp = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp();\n141\t    let file_path = media_dir.join(format!(\"media_{}_{}.{}\", message_id, timestamp, extension));\n142\t\n143\t    // Download with FLOOD_WAIT retry + timeout (2 min max)\n144\t    let download_future = with_flood_wait_retry(|| async {\n145\t        wrapper.client.download_media(&media, &file_path).await\n146\t    });\n147\t    let downloaded = tokio::time::timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(120), download_future)\n148\t        .await\n149\t [2026-06-13T13:13:35.550Z] [INFO]       "content": "//! Media download endpoints (parity with commands/media.rs). The desktop\n//! commands return local file paths; over HTTP we stream the bytes and keep\n//! a server-side disk cache.\n\nuse std::path::Path as FsPath;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse axum::extract::{Path, State};\nuse axum::http::header;\nuse axum::response::Response;\nuse axum::{Extension, Json};\nuse grammers_client::types::Message as GrammersMessage;\n\nuse crate::auth::UserId;\nuse crate::context::ServerContext;\nuse crate::error::ApiError;\nuse crate::flood::with_flood_wait_retry;\nuse crate::peer::resolve_peer;\nuse crate::routes::account_client;\n\n/// Read a cached file from disk into a bytes response.\n///\n/// We serve user-supplied bytes from the API origin, so the response is\n/// hardened against content-sniffing / stored-XSS: `nosniff` pins the\n/// declared type, a locked-down CSP (`default-src 'none'; sandbox`) prevents\n/// any markup that slips through from executing script in our origin, and\n/// `disposition` (\"inline\" for known-renderable media, \"attachment\" for\n/// everything else) stops the browser treating a download as a page.\npub(crate) async fn serve_file(\n    path: &FsPath,\n    content_type: &str,\n    disposition: &str,\n) -> Result<Response, ApiError> {\n    let bytes = tokio::fs::read(path).await.map_err(ApiError::internal)?;\n    Response::builder()\n        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, content_type)\n        .header(header::CONTENT_DISPOSITION, disposition)\n        .header(header::X_CONTENT_TYPE_OPTIONS, \"nosniff\")\n        .header(header::CONTENT_SECURITY_POLICY, \"default-src 'none'; sandbox\")\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(bytes))\n        .map_err(ApiError::internal)\n}\n\n/// Map a raw Telegram mime type to a `(content_type, disposition)` pair that\n/// is safe to serve from the API origin. Only image (excluding SVG, which can\n/// script), audio and video render inline; anything else — notably\n/// `text/html`, `image/svg+xml`, `application/xhtml+xml`, `*/*+xml` — is\n/// neutralised to an opaque download.\nfn safe_serving(raw: &str) -> (String, &'static str) {\n    let base = raw.split(';').next().unwrap_or(\"\").trim().to_ascii_lowercase();\n    let inline = (base.starts_with(\"image/\") && base != \"image/svg+xml\")\n        || base.starts_with(\"audio/\")\n        || base.starts_with(\"video/\");\n    if inline {\n        (base, \"inline\")\n    } else {\n        (\"application/octet-stream\".to_string(), \"attachment\")\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get file extension from media type (ported from commands/media.rs).\nfn media_extension(media: &grammers_client::types::Media) -> String {\n    match media {\n        grammers_client::types::Media::Photo(_) => \"jpg\".to_string(),\n        grammers_client::types::Media::Document(doc) => doc\n            .mime_type()\n            .map(|mime| match mime {\n                \"audio/ogg\" | \"audio/opus\" | \"audio/ogg; codecs=opus\" => \"ogg\",\n                m if m.starts_with(\"video/\") => \"mp4\",\n                m if m.starts_with(\"audio/\") => \"mp3\",\n                m if m.starts_with(\"image/\") => m.split('/').nth(1).unwrap_or(\"dat\"),\n                _ => \"dat\",\n            })\n            .unwrap_or(\"dat\")\n            .to_string(),\n        _ => \"dat\".to_string(),\n    }\n}\n\nfn media_content_type(media: &grammers_client::types::Media) -> String {\n    match media {\n        grammers_client::types::Media::Photo(_) => \"image/jpeg\".to_string(),\n        grammers_client::types::Media::Document(doc) => doc\n            .mime_type()\n            .unwrap_or(\"application/octet-stream\")\n            .to_string(),\n        _ => \"application/octet-stream\".to_string(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Download the media of one message and stream it back.\npub async fn download_media(\n    State(ctx): State<Arc<ServerContext>>,\n    user: Extension<UserId>,\n    Path((account_id, chat_id, message_id)): Path<(String, i64, i32)>,\n) -> Result<Response, ApiError> {\n    tracing::info!(account_id = %account_id, chat_id, message_id, \"Download media requested\");\n\n    let wrapper = account_client(&ctx, &user.0, &account_id).await?;\n    let chat = resolve_peer(&wrapper, chat_id).await?;\n\n    // Find the specific message (same window as the desktop command)\n    let mut messages_iter = wrapper\n        .client\n        .iter_messages(&chat)\n        .offset_id(message_id + 1)\n        .limit(50);\n\n    let mut target_message: Option<GrammersMessage> = None;\n    while let Some(msg) = messages_iter\n        .next()\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ApiError::telegram(format!(\"Failed to get messages: {e}\")))?\n    {\n        if msg.id() == message_id {\n            target_message = Some(msg);\n            break;\n        }\n    }\n\n    let message = target_message.ok_or_else(|| {\n        ApiError::NotFound(format!(\"Message {message_id} not found in chat {chat_id}\"))\n    })?;\n\n    let media = message\n        .media()\n        .ok_or_else(|| ApiError::NotFound(\"Message has no media\".into()))?;\n\n    // WebPage previews are not downloadable files\n    if matches!(media, grammers_client::types::Media::WebPage(_)) {\n        return Err(ApiError::NotFound(\"Message media is a link preview\".into()));\n    }\n\n    let media_dir = ctx.media_dir.join(format!(\"chat_{}\", chat_id.unsigned_abs()));\n    tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&media_dir)\n        .await\n        .map_err(ApiError::internal)?;\n\n    let extension = media_extension(&media);\n    let timestamp = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp();\n    let file_path = media_dir.join(format!(\"media_{}_{}.{}\", message_id, timestamp, extension));\n\n    // Download with FLOOD_WAIT retry + timeout (2 min max)\n    let download_future = with_flood_wait_retry(|| async {\n        wrapper.client.download_media(&media, &file_path).await\n    });\n    let downloaded = tokio::time::timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(120), download_future)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|_| ApiError::Telegram(\"Media download timed out\".into()))?;\n    downloaded.map_err(|e| ApiError::telegram(format!(\"Failed to download media: {e}\")))?;\n\n    let (content_type, disposition) = safe_serving(&media_content_type(&media));\n    serve_file(&file_path, &content_type, disposition).await\n}\n\n/// Chat/user profile photo as image bytes, cached on disk.\npub async fn chat_photo(\n    State(ctx): State<Arc<ServerContext>>,\n    user: Extension<UserId>,\n    Path((account_id, chat_id)): Path<(String, i64)>,\n) -> Result<Response, ApiError> {\n    let wrapper = account_client(&ctx, &user.0, &account_id).await?;\n\n    let avatars_dir = ctx.media_dir.join(\"avatars\");\n    tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&avatars_dir)\n        .await\n        .map_err(ApiError::internal)?;\n    let file_path = avatars_dir.join(format!(\"chat_{}.jpg\", chat_id.unsigned_abs()));\n\n    if !file_path.exists() {\n        let peer = resolve_peer(&wrapper, chat_id).await?;\n\n        let photo = with_flood_wait_retry(|| async {\n            let mut photos = wrapper.client.iter_profile_photos(&peer);\n            photos.next().await\n        })\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ApiError::telegram(format!(\"Error getting profile photos: {e}\")))?;\n\n        let Some(photo) = photo else {\n            return Err(ApiError::NotFound(\"No profile photo\".into()));\n        };\n\n        with_flood_wait_retry(|| async { wrapper.client.download_media(&photo, &file_path).await })\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| ApiError::telegram(format!(\"Failed to download profile photo: {e}\")))?;\n    }\n\n    serve_file(&file_path, \"image/jpeg\", \"inline\").await\n}\n\n#[derive(serde::Serialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"camelCase\")]\npub struct UserPhotosResponse {\n    pub count: usize,\n    pub urls: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// All profile photos: downloads them into the avatar cache and returns\n/// URLs of the per-index endpoint (parity with get_user_photos, which\n/// returns local file paths on Result<Json<UserPhotosResponse>, ApiError> {\n    let wrapper = account_client(&ctx, &user.0, &account_id).await?;\n    let peer = resolve_peer(&wrapper, chat_id).await?;\n\n    let avatars_dir = ctx.media_dir.join(\"avatars\");\n    tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&avatars_dir)\n        .await\n        .map_err(ApiError::internal)?;\n\n    let mut count = 0usize;\n    let mut photos = wrapper.client.iter_profile_photos(&peer);\n    loop {\n        match photos.next().await {\n            Ok(Some(photo)) => {\n                let file_path =\n                    avatars_dir.join(format!(\"chat_{}_photo_{}.jpg\", chat_id.unsigned_abs(), count));\n                if !file_path.exists() {\n                    if let Err(e) = wrapper.client.download_media(&photo, &file_path).await {\n                        tracing::warn!(error = %e, index = count, \"Failed to download profile photo\");\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n                count += 1;\n            }\n            Ok(None) => break,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"Error iterating profile photos\");\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let urls = (0..count)\n        .map(|i| format!(\"/api/v1/accounts/{account_id}/chats/{chat_id}/photos/{i}\"))\n        .collect();\n    Ok(Json(UserPhotosResponse { count, urls }))\n}\n\npub async fn user_photo_by_index(\n    State(ctx): State<Arc<ServerContext>>,\n    user: Extension<UserId>,\n    Path((account_id, chat_id, index)): Err(ApiError::NotFound(\n            \"Photo not cached — call the photos listing first\".into(),\n        ));\n    }\n    serve_file(&file_path, \"image/jpeg\", \"inline\").await\n}\n",

chat_id)): Path<(String, i64)>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    body: Bytes,\n) -> Result<Json<Message>, ApiError> {\n    use percent_encoding::percent_decode_str;\n\n    let header = |name: &str| -> Option<String> {\n        headers.get(name).and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()).map(|s| s.to_string())\n    };\n    let decoded = |value: String| -> Result<String, ApiError> {\n [2026-06-13T13:15:34.625Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n/**\n * vasya-mcp — MCP (stdio) server over the vasya-api Telegram session host.\n *\n * Thin by design: every tool is one REST call. Configure with:\n *   VASYA_API_URL   base URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8787)\n *   VASYA_AGENT_KEY scoped agent key (vk_...) or any bearer the API accepts\n *\n * Tool availability follows the key's scopes — out-of-scope calls return\n * the API's 403 with the missing scope named, so agents can self-diagnose.\n */\n\nimport { McpServer } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js\";\nimport { StdioServerTransport } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js\";\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\nimport { randomUUID } from \"node:crypto\";\n\nconst BASE_URL = (process.env.VASYA_API_URL ?? \"http://127.0.0.1:8787\").replace(/\\/$/, \"\");\nconst AGENT_KEY = process.env.VASYA_AGENT_KEY;\nif (!AGENT_KEY) {\n  console.error(\"VASYA_AGENT_KEY is required (create one via POST /api/v1/agent-keys)\");\n  process.exit(1);\n}\n\nasync function api(\n  method: string,\n  path: string,\n  body?: unknown,\n  idempotent = false,\n): Promise<string> {\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = { Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}` };\n  if (body !== undefined) headers[\"content-type\"] = \"application/json\";\n  // Safe retries for mutations: the server replays on duplicate keys.\n  if (idempotent) headers[\"Idempotency-Key\"] = randomUUID();\n\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    method,\n    headers,\n    body: body === undefined ? undefined : JSON.stringify(body),\n  });\n\n  const text = await response.text();\n  if (!response.ok) {\n    const retryAfter = response.headers.get(\"retry-after\");\n    throw new Error(\n      `${method} ${path} -> ${response.status}${retryAfter ? ` (retry after ${retryAfter}s)` : \"\"}: ${text}`,\n    );\n  }\n  return text.length > 0 ? text : JSON.stringify({ ok: true });\n}\n\nconst asText = (text: string) => ({ content: [{ type: \"text\" as const, text }] });\n\nconst server = new McpServer({ name: \"vasya-mcp\", version: \"0.1.0\" });\n\nconst accountId = z.string().describe(\"Telegram account id (from list_accounts)\");\nconst chatId = z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Chat id (bot-api format, from list_chats)\");\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_accounts\",\n  \"List the connected Telegram accounts (id, phone, connected). Account ids are required by every other tool.\",\n  {},\n  async () => asText(await api(\"GET\", \"/accounts\")),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_chats\",\n  \"List an account's chats (id, title, unread count, type user/group/channel, last message preview). Set live=true to force a fresh fetch from Telegram instead of the cache.\",\n  { accountId, live: z.boolean().optional().describe(\"Bypass the cache (slower)\") },\n  async ({ accountId, live }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats${live ? \"?source=live\" : \"\"}`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_contacts\",\n  \"List contacts: chats of type 'user' for an account.\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/contacts`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_messages\",\n  \"Read messages from a chat, newest first. Use offsetId for pagination (pass the lowest message id from the previous page). topicId reads one forum topic.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Max messages (default 50)\"),\n    offsetId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Only messages older than this id\"),\n    topicId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Forum topic id (from get_forum_topics)\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, limit, offsetId, topicId }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    if (offsetId !== undefined) params.set(\"offset_id\", String(offsetId));\n    if (topicId !== undefined) params.set(\"topic_id\", String(topicId));\n    const qs = params.size > 0 ? `?${params}` : \"\";\n    return asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages${qs}`));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"send_message\",\n  \"Send a text message to a chat (optionally into a forum topic). Returns the sent message. Rate-limited per account — on 429, wait the indicated seconds.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    text: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Message text\"),\n    topicId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Forum topic id\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, text, topicId }) =>\n    asText(\n      await api(\"POST\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages`, { text, topicId }, true),\n    ),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"forward_messages\",\n  \"Forward messages between chats. Returns the new message ids (null entries failed).\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    fromChatId: z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Source chat id\"),\n    toChatId: z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Destination chat id\"),\n    messageIds: z.array(z.coerce.number().int()).min(1).describe(\"Message ids to forward\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, fromChatId, toChatId, messageIds }) =>\n    asText(\n      await api(\n        \"POST\",\n        `/accounts/${accountId}/messages/forward`,\n        { fromChatId, toChatId, messageIds },\n        true,\n      ),\n    ),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"mark_messages_read\",\n  \"Mark messages as read in a chat up to a message id (sends the read receipt to Telegram).\",\n  { accountId, chatId, maxId: z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Highest message id to mark read\") },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, maxId }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"POST\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/read`, { maxId }, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"search_messages\",\n  \"Full-text search within one chat.\",\n  { accountId, chatId, query: z.string().min(1), limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional() },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, query, limit }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams({ q: query });\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    return asText(\n      await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages/search?${params}`),\n    );\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"search_all_messages\",\n  \"Full-text search across all chats of an account. Returns message previews with chat titles.\",\n  { accountId, query: z.string().min(1), limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional() },\n  async ({ accountId, query, limit }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams({ q: query });\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    return asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/messages/search?${params}`));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"global_search\",\n  \"Search Telegram globally for users, groups and channels by name/username (not message content).\",\n  { accountId, query: z.string().min(1), limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional() },\n  async ({ accountId, query, limit }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams({ q: query });\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    return asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/search?${params}`));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_forum_topics\",\n  \"List the topics of a forum supergroup (chats with isForum=true).\",\n  { accountId, chatId },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/topics`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_folders\",\n  \"List the account's UI folders (chat organization).\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/folders`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"start_loading_chats\",\n  \"Trigger a background refresh of the chat list from Telegram. Results land in the cache used by list_chats; poll list_chats afterwards.\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"POST\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/load`)),\n);\n\nconst transport = new StdioServerTransport();\nawait server.connect(transport);\nconsole.error(`vasya-mcp ready (api: ${BASE_URL})`);\n",
