[2026-06-13T13:15:51.667Z] [INFO]           "new_string": "const asText = (text: string) => ({ content: [{ type: \"text\" as const, text }] });\n\n/** Build the API error message exactly like `api()` does (status + body). */\nfunction apiError(method: string, path: string, response: Response, body: string): Error {\n  const retryAfter = response.headers.get(\"retry-after\");\n  return new Error(\n    `${method} ${path} -> ${response.status}${retryAfter ? ` (retry after ${retryAfter}s)` : \"\"}: ${body}`,\n  );\n}\n\n/** Pick a file extension for a downloaded blob from its content-type. */\nfunction extFromContentType(contentType: string): string {\n  const base = contentType.split(\";\")[0]?.trim().toLowerCase() ?? \"\";\n  const known: Record<string, string> = {\n    \"image/jpeg\": \"jpg\",\n    \"image/png\": \"png\",\n    \"image/gif\": \"gif\",\n    \"image/webp\": \"webp\",\n    \"audio/mpeg\": \"mp3\",\n    \"audio/ogg\": \"ogg\",\n    \"video/mp4\": \"mp4\",\n    \"application/octet-stream\": \"bin\",\n  };\n  if (known[base]) return known[base];\n  const sub = base.split(\"/\")[1];\n  return sub ? sub.replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"\") || \"bin\" : \"bin\";\n}\n\n/**\n * GET a binary endpoint (media/avatars/photos) and return it either as a saved\n * file path (default — keeps the agent context small) or inline base64. Images\n * requested as base64 come back as an MCP image block the model can view.\n */\nasync function fetchBinary(path: string, mode: \"path\" | \"base64\", name: string) {\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}` },\n  });\n  const buffer = Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer());\n  if (!response.ok) {\n    throw apiError(\"GET\", path, response, buffer.toString(\"utf8\"));\n  }\n  const contentType = response.headers.get(\"content-type\") ?? \"application/octet-stream\";\n\n  if (mode === \"base64\") {\n    const data = buffer.toString(\"base64\");\n    if (contentType.split(\";\")[0]?.trim().startsWith(\"image/\")) {\n      return {\n        content: [\n          { type: \"image\" as const, data, mimeType: contentType.split(\";\")[0].trim() },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n    return asText(JSON.stringify({ contentType, bytes: buffer.length, base64: data }));\n  }\n\n  await mkdir(DOWNLOAD_DIR, { recursive: true });\n  const dest = join(DOWNLOAD_DIR, `${name}_${randomUUID()}.${extFromContentType(contentType)}`);\n  await writeFile(dest, buffer);\n  return asText(JSON.stringify({ path: dest, contentType, bytes: buffer.length }));\n}\n\n/** Percent-encode a header value the way the server's `send_media` decodes it. */\nconst encodeHeader = (value: string) => encodeURIComponent(value);\n\n/**\n * POST raw file bytes to the media endpoint: body is the bytes, metadata rides\n * in percent-encoded `x-*` headers (mirrors `src/transport/http.ts` sendMedia).\n */\nasync function sendMedia(\n  accountId: string,\n  chatId: number,\n  bytes: Buffer,\n  meta: { fileName: string; mimeType?: string; caption?: string },\n): Promise<string> {\n  const path = `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/media`;\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = {\n    Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}`,\n    \"content-type\": \"application/octet-stream\",\n    \"Idempotency-Key\": randomUUID(),\n    \"x-file-name\": encodeHeader(meta.fileName),\n  };\n  if (meta.mimeType) headers[\"x-mime-type\"] = meta.mimeType;\n  if (meta.caption !== undefined) headers[\"x-caption\"] = encodeHeader(meta.caption);\n\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    method: \"POST\",\n    headers,\n    body: bytes as unknown as BodyInit,\n  });\n  const text = await response.text();\n  if (!response.ok) throw apiError(\"POST\", path, response, text);\n  return text.length > 0 ? text : JSON.stringify({ ok: true });\n}\n\nconst server = new McpServer({ name: \"vasya-mcp\", version: \"0.1.0\" });"

[2026-06-13T13:15:51.722Z] [INFO]     "newString": "const asText = (text: string) => ({ content: [{ type: \"text\" as const, text }] });\n\n/** Build the API error message exactly like `api()` does (status + body). */\nfunction apiError(method: string, path: string, response: Response, body: string): Error {\n  const retryAfter = response.headers.get(\"retry-after\");\n  return new Error(\n    `${method} ${path} -> ${response.status}${retryAfter ? ` (retry after ${retryAfter}s)` : \"\"}: ${body}`,\n  );\n}\n\n/** Pick a file extension for a downloaded blob from its content-type. */\nfunction extFromContentType(contentType: string): string {\n  const base = contentType.split(\";\")[0]?.trim().toLowerCase() ?? \"\";\n  const known: Record<string, string> = {\n    \"image/jpeg\": \"jpg\",\n    \"image/png\": \"png\",\n    \"image/gif\": \"gif\",\n    \"image/webp\": \"webp\",\n    \"audio/mpeg\": \"mp3\",\n    \"audio/ogg\": \"ogg\",\n    \"video/mp4\": \"mp4\",\n    \"application/octet-stream\": \"bin\",\n  };\n  if (known[base]) return known[base];\n  const sub = base.split(\"/\")[1];\n  return sub ? sub.replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"\") || \"bin\" : \"bin\";\n}\n\n/**\n * GET a binary endpoint (media/avatars/photos) and return it either as a saved\n * file path (default — keeps the agent context small) or inline base64. Images\n * requested as base64 come back as an MCP image block the model can view.\n */\nasync function fetchBinary(path: string, mode: \"path\" | \"base64\", name: string) {\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}` },\n  });\n  const buffer = Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer());\n  if (!response.ok) {\n    throw apiError(\"GET\", path, response, buffer.toString(\"utf8\"));\n  }\n  const contentType = response.headers.get(\"content-type\") ?? \"application/octet-stream\";\n\n  if (mode === \"base64\") {\n    const data = buffer.toString(\"base64\");\n    if (contentType.split(\";\")[0]?.trim().startsWith(\"image/\")) {\n      return {\n        content: [\n          { type: \"image\" as const, data, mimeType: contentType.split(\";\")[0].trim() },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n    return asText(JSON.stringify({ contentType, bytes: buffer.length, base64: data }));\n  }\n\n  await mkdir(DOWNLOAD_DIR, { recursive: true });\n  const dest = join(DOWNLOAD_DIR, `${name}_${randomUUID()}.${extFromContentType(contentType)}`);\n  await writeFile(dest, buffer);\n  return asText(JSON.stringify({ path: dest, contentType, bytes: buffer.length }));\n}\n\n/** Percent-encode a header value the way the server's `send_media` decodes it. */\nconst encodeHeader = (value: string) => encodeURIComponent(value);\n\n/**\n * POST raw file bytes to the media endpoint: body is the bytes, metadata rides\n * in percent-encoded `x-*` headers (mirrors `src/transport/http.ts` sendMedia).\n */\nasync function sendMedia(\n  accountId: string,\n  chatId: number,\n  bytes: Buffer,\n  meta: { fileName: string; mimeType?: string; caption?: string },\n): Promise<string> {\n  const path = `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/media`;\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = {\n    Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}`,\n    \"content-type\": \"application/octet-stream\",\n    \"Idempotency-Key\": randomUUID(),\n    \"x-file-name\": encodeHeader(meta.fileName),\n  };\n  if (meta.mimeType) headers[\"x-mime-type\"] = meta.mimeType;\n  if (meta.caption !== undefined) headers[\"x-caption\"] = encodeHeader(meta.caption);\n\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    method: \"POST\",\n    headers,\n    body: bytes as unknown as BodyInit,\n  });\n  const text = await response.text();\n  if (!response.ok) throw apiError(\"POST\", path, response, text);\n  return text.length > 0 ? text : JSON.stringify({ ok: true });\n}\n\nconst server = new McpServer({ name: \"vasya-mcp\", version: \"0.1.0\" });",
[2026-06-13T13:15:51.722Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n/**\n * vasya-mcp — MCP (stdio) server over the vasya-api Telegram session host.\n *\n * Thin by design: every tool is one REST call. Configure with:\n *   VASYA_API_URL   base URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8787)\n *   VASYA_AGENT_KEY scoped agent key (vk_...) or any bearer the API accepts\n *\n * Tool availability follows the key's scopes — out-of-scope calls return\n * the API's 403 with the missing scope named, so agents can self-diagnose.\n */\n\nimport { McpServer } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js\";\nimport { StdioServerTransport } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js\";\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\nimport { randomUUID } from \"node:crypto\";\nimport { readFile, writeFile, mkdir } from \"node:fs/promises\";\nimport { basename, join } from \"node:path\";\nimport { tmpdir } from \"node:os\";\n\nconst BASE_URL = (process.env.VASYA_API_URL ?? \"http://127.0.0.1:8787\").replace(/\\/$/, \"\");\nconst AGENT_KEY = process.env.VASYA_AGENT_KEY;\nif (!AGENT_KEY) {\n  console.error(\"VASYA_AGENT_KEY is required (create one via POST /api/v1/agent-keys)\");\n  process.exit(1);\n}\n\n// Where download_media / get_*_avatar / get_chat_photo write files when the\n// caller asks for a path (the default) instead of inline base64.\nconst DOWNLOAD_DIR = process.env.VASYA_DOWNLOAD_DIR ?? join(tmpdir(), \"vasya-mcp-downloads\");\n\nasync function api(\n  method: string,\n  path: string,\n  body?: unknown,\n  idempotent = false,\n): Promise<string> {\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = { Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}` };\n  if (body !== undefined) headers[\"content-type\"] = \"application/json\";\n  // Safe retries for mutations: the server replays on duplicate keys.\n  if (idempotent) headers[\"Idempotency-Key\"] = randomUUID();\n\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    method,\n    headers,\n    body: body === undefined ? undefined : JSON.stringify(body),\n  });\n\n  const text = await response.text();\n  if (!response.ok) {\n    const retryAfter = response.headers.get(\"retry-after\");\n    throw new Error(\n      `${method} ${path} -> ${response.status}${retryAfter ? ` (retry after ${retryAfter}s)` : \"\"}: ${text}`,\n    );\n  }\n  return text.length > 0 ? text : JSON.stringify({ ok: true });\n}\n\nconst asText = (text: string) => ({ content: [{ type: \"text\" as const, text }] });\n\nconst server = new McpServer({ name: \"vasya-mcp\", version: \"0.1.0\" });\n\nconst accountId = z.string().describe(\"Telegram account id (from list_accounts)\");\nconst chatId = z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Chat id (bot-api format, from list_chats)\");\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_accounts\",\n  \"List the connected Telegram accounts (id, phone, connected). Account ids are required by every other tool.\",\n  {},\n  async () => asText(await api(\"GET\", \"/accounts\")),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_chats\",\n  \"List an account's chats (id, title, unread count, type user/group/channel, last message preview). Set live=true to force a fresh fetch from Telegram instead of the cache.\",\n  { accountId, live: z.boolean().optional().describe(\"Bypass the cache (slower)\") },\n  async ({ accountId, live }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats${live ? \"?source=live\" : \"\"}`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_contacts\",\n  \"List contacts: chats of type 'user' for an account.\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/contacts`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_messages\",\n  \"Read messages from a chat, newest first. Use offsetId for pagination (pass the lowest message id from the previous page). topicId reads one forum topic.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Max messages (default 50)\"),\n    offsetId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Only messages older than this id\"),\n    topicId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Forum topic id (from get_forum_topics)\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, limit, offsetId, topicId }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    if (offsetId !== undefined) params.set(\"offset_id\", String(offsetId));\n    if (topicId !== undefined) params.set(\"topic_id\", String(topicId));\n    const qs = params.size > 0 ? `?${params}` : \"\";\n    return asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages${qs}`));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"send_message\",\n  \"Send a text message to a chat (optionally into a forum topic). Returns the sent message. Rate-limited per account — on 429, wait the indicated seconds.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    text: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Message text\"),\n    topicId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Forum topic id\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, text, topicId }) =>\n    asText(\n      await api(\"POST\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages`, { text, topicId }, true),\n    ),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"forward_messages\",\n  \"Forward messages between chats. Returns the new message ids (null entries failed).\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    fromChatId: z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Source chat id\"),\n    toChatId: z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Destination chat id\"),\n    messageIds: z.array(z.coerce.number().int()).min(1).describe(\"Message ids to forward\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, fromChatId, toChatId, messageIds }) =>\n    asText(\n      await api(\n        \"POST\",\n        `/accounts/${accountId}/messages/forward`,\n        { fromChatId, toChatId, messageIds },\n        true,\n      ),\n    ),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"mark_messages_read\",\n  \"Mark messages as read in a chat up to a message id (sends the read receipt to Telegram).\",\n  { accountId, chatId, maxId: z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Highest message id to mark read\") },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, maxId }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"POST\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/read`, { maxId }, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"search_messages\",\n  \"Full-text search within one chat.\",\n  { accountId, chatId, query: z.string().min(1), limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional() },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, query, limit }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams({ q: query });\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    return asText(\n      await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages/search?${params}`),\n    );\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"search_all_messages\",\n  \"Full-text search across all chats of an account. Returns message previews with chat titles.\",\n  { accountId, query: z.string().min(1), limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional() },\n  async ({ accountId, query, limit }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams({ q: query });\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    return asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/messages/search?${params}`));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"global_search\",\n  \"Search Telegram globally for users, groups and channels by name/username (not message content).\",\n  { accountId, query: z.string().min(1), limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional() },\n  async ({ accountId, query, limit }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams({ q: query });\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    return asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/search?${params}`));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_forum_topics\",\n  \"List the topics of a forum supergroup (chats with isForum=true).\",\n  { accountId, chatId },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/topics`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_folders\",\n  \"List the account's UI folders (chat organization).\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/folders`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"start_loading_chats\",\n  \"Trigger a background refresh of the chat list from Telegram. Results land in the cache used by list_chats; poll list_chats afterwards.\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"POST\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/load`)),\n);\n\nconst transport = new StdioServerTransport();\nawait server.connect(transport);\nconsole.error(`vasya-mcp ready (api: ${BASE_URL})`);\n",

Continue with verify_login_code using that accountId. Requires server-side API credentials.\",\n  { phone: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Phone number in international format, e.g. +12025550123\") },\n  async ({ phone }) => asText(await api(\"POST\", \"/telegram/login/code\", { phone }, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"verify_login_code\",\n  \"Submit the login code Telegram sent. Returns {status:'authorized', user} on success or {status:'password_required'} if the account has 2FA — then call submit_2fa_password (scope: telegram:login).\",\n  {\n    accountId: z.string().describe(\"accountId from request_login_code\"),\n    code: z.string().min(1).describe(\"The login code from SMS/Telegram app\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, code }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"POST\", \"/telegram/login/verify\", { accountId, code }, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"submit_2fa_password\",\n  \"Finish a login that returned password_required by submitting the account's 2FA (cloud) password (scope: telegram:login).\",\n  {\n    accountId: z.string().describe(\"accountId from request_login_code\"),\n    password: z.string().min(1).describe(\"The 2FA cloud password\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, password }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"POST\", \"/telegram/login/password\", { accountId, password }, true)),\n);\n\n// --- Accounts ----------------------------------------------------------------\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"delete_account\",\n  \"Log out and remove a connected account from the server (scope: telegram:login). Irreversible — the session is dropped; logging back in needs the code flow again.\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"DELETE\", `/accounts/${accountId}`, undefined, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_account_avatar\",\n  \"Fetch the account owner's own profile photo (scope: accounts:read). Returns a saved file path by default, or set output='base64' to get the image inline.\",\n  { accountId, output: binaryOutput },\n  async ({ accountId, output }) =>\n    fetchBinary(`/accounts/${accountId}/avatar`, output, `avatar_${accountId}`),\n);\n\n// --- Chats -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"create_group\",\n  \"Create a basic Telegram group with the given title and initial members (scope: chats:write). Returns the new chat id (bot-api format).\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    title: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Group title\"),\n    userIds: z\n      .array(z.coerce.number().int())\n      .min(1)\n      .describe(\"User ids to add (from get_contacts/global_search)\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, title, userIds }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"POST\", `/accounts/${accountId}/groups`, { title, userIds }, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"create_channel\",\n  \"Create a broadcast channel, or a supergroup when isMegagroup=true (scope: chats:write). Returns the new chat id (bot-api format).\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    title: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Channel/supergroup title\"),\n    about: z.string().optional().describe(\"Description (optional)\"),\n    isMegagroup: z\n      .boolean()\n      .optional()\n      .describe(\"true → supergroup, false/omitted → broadcast channel\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, title, about, isMegagroup }) =>\n    asText(\n      await api(\n        \"POST\",\n        `/accounts/${accountId}/channels`,\n        { title, about, isMegagroup },\n        true,\n      ),\n    ),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"delete_chat\",\n  \"Delete/leave a chat (scope: chats:write). Channels/supergroups are left, basic groups are left, and user chats have their history cleared.\",\n  { accountId, chatId },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"DELETE\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}`, undefined, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_chat_photo\",\n  \"Fetch a chat/user's current profile photo (scope: chats:read). Folders organize the chat list by type and explicit include/exclude lists.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    id: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Folder id (stable key; reuse to update)\"),\n    name: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Folder display name\"),\n    icon: z.string().optional().describe(\"Optional icon name/emoji\"),\n    includedChatTypes: z\n      .array(z.string())\n      .optional()\n      .describe(\"Chat types to include, e.g. ['user','group','channel']\"),\n    excludedChatTypes: z.array(z.string()).optional().describe(\"Chat types to exclude\"),\n    includedChatIds: z.array(z.coerce.number().int()).optional().describe(\"Specific chat ids to include\"),\n    excludedChatIds: z.array(z.coerce.number().int()).optional().describe(\"Specific chat ids to exclude\"),\n    sortOrder: z.coerce.number().int().min(0).max(10000).describe(\"Sort position (0–10000)\"),\n  },\n  async ({\n    accountId,\n    id,\n    name,\n    icon,\n    includedChatTypes,\n    excludedChatTypes,\n    includedChatIds,\n    excludedChatIds,\n    sortOrder,\n  }) =>\n    asText(\n      await api(\n        \"POST\",\n        `/accounts/${accountId}/folders`,\n        {\n          // FolderRecord is snake_case on the wire (no camelCase rename).\n          id,\n          account_id: accountId,\n          name,\n          icon: icon ?? null,\n          included_chat_types: includedChatTypes ?? [],\n          excluded_chat_types: excludedChatTypes ?? [],\n          included_chat_ids: includedChatIds ?? [],\n          excluded_chat_ids: excludedChatIds ?? [],\n          sort_order: sortOrder,\n        },\n        true,\n      ),\n    ),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"delete_folder\",\n  \"Delete a UI folder by id (scope: folders:write).\",\n  { accountId, folderId: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Folder id to delete\") },\n  async ({ accountId, folderId }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"DELETE\", `/accounts/${accountId}/folders/${folderId}`, undefined, true)),\n);\n\n// --- Tabs --------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_tabs\",\n  \"List the account's UI tabs (visibility + order; scope: folders:read).\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/tabs`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"save_tabs\",\n  \"Replace the account's full set of UI tabs (scope: folders:write). Send the complete list; it overwrites what's stored.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    tabs: z\n      .array(\n        z.object({\n          id: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Tab id\"),\n          visible: z.boolean().describe(\"Whether the tab is shown\"),\n          sortOrder: z.coerce.number().int().min(0).max(10000).describe(\"Sort position (0–10000)\"),\n        }),\n      )\n      .describe(\"The complete ordered list of tabs\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, tabs }) =>\n    asText(\n      await api(\n        \"PUT\",\n        `/accounts/${accountId}/tabs`,\n        // TabRecord is snake_case on the wire.\n        tabs.map((t) => ({\n          id: t.id,\n          account_id: accountId,\n          visible: t.visible,\n          sort_order: t.sortOrder,\n        })),\n        true,\n      ),\n    ),\n);\n\n// --- Media -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"send_media\",\n  \"Upload and send a file to a chat (scope: messages:send). Provide either a local 'path' or base64 'data' (then 'fileName' is required). Optional caption and mimeType. Returns the sent message.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    path: z.string().optional().describe(\"Local file path to read and send\"),\n    data: z.string().optional().describe(\"Base64 file contents (alternative to path)\"),\n    fileName: z\n      .string()\n      .optional()\n      .describe(\"File name; required with 'data', defaults to path's basename\"),\n    caption: z.string().optional().describe(\"Optional caption text\"),\n    mimeType: z\n      .string()\n      .optional()\n      .describe(\"MIME type; image/* sends as a photo, otherwise as a document\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, path, data, fileName, caption, mimeType }) => {\n    let bytes: Buffer;\n    let name: string;\n    if (path !== undefined) {\n      bytes = await readFile(path);\n      name = fileName ?? basename(path);\n    } else if (data !== undefined) {\n      if (!fileName) throw new Error(\"fileName is required when sending base64 data\");\n      bytes = Buffer.from(data, \"base64\");\n      name = fileName;\n    } else {\n      throw new Error(\"Provide either 'path' (local file) or 'data' (base64)\");\n    }\n    if (bytes.length === 0) throw new Error(\"Refusing to send an empty file\");\n    return asText(await sendMedia(accountId, chatId, bytes, { fileName: name, mimeType, caption }));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"download_media\",\n  \"Download a message's media (scope: messages:read). Returns a saved file path by default, or set output='base64' for the bytes inline (images come back as a viewable image).\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    messageId: z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Message id whose media to download\"),\n    output: binaryOutput,\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, messageId, output }) =>\n    fetchBinary(\n      `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages/${messageId}/media`,\n      output,\n      `media_${chatId}_${messageId}`,\n    ),\n);\n\nconst transport = new StdioServerTransport();\nawait server.connect(transport);\nconsole.error(`vasya-mcp ready (api: ${BASE_URL})`);"

Returns the sent message.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    path: z.string().optional().describe(\"Local file path to read and send\"),\n    data: z.string().optional().describe(\"Base64 file contents (alternative to path)\"),\n    fileName: z\n      .string()\n      .optional()\n      .describe(\"File name; required with 'data', defaults to path's basename\"),\n    caption: z.string().optional().describe(\"Optional caption text\"),\n    mimeType: z\n      .string()\n      .optional()\n      .describe(\"MIME type; image/* sends as a photo, otherwise as a document\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, path, data, fileName, caption, mimeType }) => {\n    let bytes: Buffer;\n    let name: string;\n    if (path !== undefined) {\n      bytes = await readFile(path);\n      name = fileName ?? basename(path);\n    } else if (data !== undefined) {\n      if (!fileName) throw new Error(\"fileName is required when sending base64 data\");\n      bytes = Buffer.from(data, \"base64\");\n      name = fileName;\n    } else {\n      throw new Error(\"Provide either 'path' (local file) or 'data' (base64)\");\n    }\n    if (bytes.length === 0) throw new Error(\"Refusing to send an empty file\");\n    return asText(await sendMedia(accountId, chatId, bytes, { fileName: name, mimeType, caption }));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"download_media\",\n  \"Download a message's media (scope: messages:read). Returns a saved file path by default, or set output='base64' for the bytes inline (images come back as a viewable image).\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    messageId: z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Message id whose media to download\"),\n    output: binaryOutput,\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, messageId, output }) =>\n    fetchBinary(\n      `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages/${messageId}/media`,\n      output,\n      `media_${chatId}_${messageId}`,\n    ),\n);\n\nconst transport = new StdioServerTransport();\nawait server.connect(transport);\nconsole.error(`vasya-mcp ready (api: ${BASE_URL})`);",
[2026-06-13T13:16:30.214Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n/**\n * vasya-mcp — MCP (stdio) server over the vasya-api Telegram session host.\n *\n * Thin by design: every tool is one REST call. Configure with:\n *   VASYA_API_URL   base URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8787)\n *   VASYA_AGENT_KEY scoped agent key (vk_...) or any bearer the API accepts\n *\n * Tool availability follows the key's scopes — out-of-scope calls return\n * the API's 403 with the missing scope named, so agents can self-diagnose.\n */\n\nimport { McpServer } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js\";\nimport { StdioServerTransport } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js\";\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\nimport { randomUUID } from \"node:crypto\";\nimport { readFile, writeFile, mkdir } from \"node:fs/promises\";\nimport { basename, join } from \"node:path\";\nimport { tmpdir } from \"node:os\";\n\nconst BASE_URL = (process.env.VASYA_API_URL ?? \"http://127.0.0.1:8787\").replace(/\\/$/, \"\");\nconst AGENT_KEY = process.env.VASYA_AGENT_KEY;\nif (!AGENT_KEY) {\n  console.error(\"VASYA_AGENT_KEY is required (create one via POST /api/v1/agent-keys)\");\n  process.exit(1);\n}\n\n// Where download_media / get_*_avatar / get_chat_photo write files when the\n// caller asks for a path (the default) instead of inline base64.\nconst DOWNLOAD_DIR = process.env.VASYA_DOWNLOAD_DIR ?? join(tmpdir(), \"vasya-mcp-downloads\");\n\nasync function api(\n  method: string,\n  path: string,\n  body?: unknown,\n  idempotent = false,\n): Promise<string> {\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = { Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}` };\n  if (body !== undefined) headers[\"content-type\"] = \"application/json\";\n  // Safe retries for mutations: the server replays on duplicate keys.\n  if (idempotent) headers[\"Idempotency-Key\"] = randomUUID();\n\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    method,\n    headers,\n    body: body === undefined ? undefined : JSON.stringify(body),\n  });\n\n  const text = await response.text();\n  if (!response.ok) {\n    const retryAfter = response.headers.get(\"retry-after\");\n    throw new Error(\n      `${method} ${path} -> ${response.status}${retryAfter ? ` (retry after ${retryAfter}s)` : \"\"}: ${text}`,\n    );\n  }\n  return text.length > 0 ? text : JSON.stringify({ ok: true });\n}\n\nconst asText = (text: string) => ({ content: [{ type: \"text\" as const, text }] });\n\n/** Build the API error message exactly like `api()` does (status + body). */\nfunction apiError(method: string, path: string, response: Response, body: string): Error {\n  const retryAfter = response.headers.get(\"retry-after\");\n  return new Error(\n    `${method} ${path} -> ${response.status}${retryAfter ? ` (retry after ${retryAfter}s)` : \"\"}: ${body}`,\n  );\n}\n\n/** Pick a file extension for a downloaded blob from its content-type. */\nfunction extFromContentType(contentType: string): string {\n  const base = contentType.split(\";\")[0]?.trim().toLowerCase() ?? \"\";\n  const known: Record<string, string> = {\n    \"image/jpeg\": \"jpg\",\n    \"image/png\": \"png\",\n    \"image/gif\": \"gif\",\n    \"image/webp\": \"webp\",\n    \"audio/mpeg\": \"mp3\",\n    \"audio/ogg\": \"ogg\",\n    \"video/mp4\": \"mp4\",\n    \"application/octet-stream\": \"bin\",\n  };\n  if (known[base]) return known[base];\n  const sub = base.split(\"/\")[1];\n  return sub ? sub.replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"\") || \"bin\" : \"bin\";\n}\n\n/**\n * GET a binary endpoint (media/avatars/photos) and return it either as a saved\n * file path (default — keeps the agent context small) or inline base64. Images\n * requested as base64 come back as an MCP image block the model can view.\n */\nasync function fetchBinary(path: string, mode: \"path\" | \"base64\", name: string) {\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}` },\n  });\n  const buffer = Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer());\n  if (!response.ok) {\n    throw apiError(\"GET\", path, response, buffer.toString(\"utf8\"));\n  }\n  const contentType = response.headers.get(\"content-type\") ?? \"application/octet-stream\";\n\n  if (mode === \"base64\") {\n    const data = buffer.toString(\"base64\");\n    if (contentType.split(\";\")[0]?.trim().startsWith(\"image/\")) {\n      return {\n        content: [\n          { type: \"image\" as const, data, mimeType: contentType.split(\";\")[0].trim() },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n    return asText(JSON.stringify({ contentType, bytes: buffer.length, base64: data }));\n  }\n\n  await mkdir(DOWNLOAD_DIR, { recursive: true });\n  const dest = join(DOWNLOAD_DIR, `${name}_${randomUUID()}.${extFromContentType(contentType)}`);\n  await writeFile(dest, buffer);\n  return asText(JSON.stringify({ path: dest, contentType, bytes: buffer.length }));\n}\n\n/** Percent-encode a header value the way the server's `send_media` decodes it. */\nconst encodeHeader = (value: string) => encodeURIComponent(value);\n\n/**\n * POST raw file bytes to the media endpoint: body is the bytes, metadata rides\n * in percent-encoded `x-*` headers (mirrors `src/transport/http.ts` sendMedia).\n */\nasync function sendMedia(\n  accountId: string,\n  chatId: number,\n  bytes: Buffer,\n  meta: { fileName: string; mimeType?: string; caption?: string },\n): Promise<string> {\n  const path = `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/media`;\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = {\n    Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}`,\n    \"content-type\": \"application/octet-stream\",\n    \"Idempotency-Key\": randomUUID(),\n    \"x-file-name\": encodeHeader(meta.fileName),\n  };\n  if (meta.mimeType) headers[\"x-mime-type\"] = meta.mimeType;\n  if (meta.caption !== undefined) headers[\"x-caption\"] = encodeHeader(meta.caption);\n\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    method: \"POST\",\n    headers,\n    body: bytes as unknown as BodyInit,\n  });\n  const text = await response.text();\n  if (!response.ok) throw apiError(\"POST\", path, response, text);\n  return text.length > 0 ? text : JSON.stringify({ ok: true });\n}\n\nconst server = new McpServer({ name: \"vasya-mcp\", version: \"0.1.0\" });\n\nconst accountId = z.string().describe(\"Telegram account id (from list_accounts)\");\nconst chatId = z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Chat id (bot-api format, from list_chats)\");\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_accounts\",\n  \"List the connected Telegram accounts (id, phone, connected). Account ids are required by every other tool.\",\n  {},\n  async () => asText(await api(\"GET\", \"/accounts\")),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_chats\",\n  \"List an account's chats (id, title, unread count, type user/group/channel, last message preview). [2026-06-13T13:16:41.741Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env node\n/**\n * vasya-mcp — MCP (stdio) server over the vasya-api Telegram session host.\n *\n * Thin by design: every tool is one REST call. Configure with:\n *   VASYA_API_URL   base URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8787)\n *   VASYA_AGENT_KEY scoped agent key (vk_...) or any bearer the API accepts\n *\n * Tool availability follows the key's scopes — out-of-scope calls return\n * the API's 403 with the missing scope named, so agents can self-diagnose.\n */\n\nimport { McpServer } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js\";\nimport { StdioServerTransport } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js\";\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\nimport { randomUUID } from \"node:crypto\";\nimport { readFile, writeFile, mkdir } from \"node:fs/promises\";\nimport { basename, join } from \"node:path\";\nimport { tmpdir } from \"node:os\";\n\nconst BASE_URL = (process.env.VASYA_API_URL ?? \"http://127.0.0.1:8787\").replace(/\\/$/, \"\");\nconst AGENT_KEY = process.env.VASYA_AGENT_KEY;\nif (!AGENT_KEY) {\n  console.error(\"VASYA_AGENT_KEY is required (create one via POST /api/v1/agent-keys)\");\n  process.exit(1);\n}\n\n// Where download_media / get_*_avatar / get_chat_photo write files when the\n// caller asks for a path (the default) instead of inline base64.\nconst DOWNLOAD_DIR = process.env.VASYA_DOWNLOAD_DIR ?? join(tmpdir(), \"vasya-mcp-downloads\");\n\nasync function api(\n  method: string,\n  path: string,\n  body?: unknown,\n  idempotent = false,\n): Promise<string> {\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = { Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}` };\n  if (body !== undefined) headers[\"content-type\"] = \"application/json\";\n  // Safe retries for mutations: the server replays on duplicate keys.\n  if (idempotent) headers[\"Idempotency-Key\"] = randomUUID();\n\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    method,\n    headers,\n    body: body === undefined ? undefined : JSON.stringify(body),\n  });\n\n  const text = await response.text();\n  if (!response.ok) {\n    const retryAfter = response.headers.get(\"retry-after\");\n    throw new Error(\n      `${method} ${path} -> ${response.status}${retryAfter ? ` (retry after ${retryAfter}s)` : \"\"}: ${text}`,\n    );\n  }\n  return text.length > 0 ? text : JSON.stringify({ ok: true });\n}\n\nconst asText = (text: string) => ({ content: [{ type: \"text\" as const, text }] });\n\n/** Build the API error message exactly like `api()` does (status + body). */\nfunction apiError(method: string, path: string, response: Response, body: string): Error {\n  const retryAfter = response.headers.get(\"retry-after\");\n  return new Error(\n    `${method} ${path} -> ${response.status}${retryAfter ? ` (retry after ${retryAfter}s)` : \"\"}: ${body}`,\n  );\n}\n\n/** Pick a file extension for a downloaded blob from its content-type. */\nfunction extFromContentType(contentType: string): string {\n  const base = contentType.split(\";\")[0]?.trim().toLowerCase() ?? \"\";\n  const known: Record<string, string> = {\n    \"image/jpeg\": \"jpg\",\n    \"image/png\": \"png\",\n    \"image/gif\": \"gif\",\n    \"image/webp\": \"webp\",\n    \"audio/mpeg\": \"mp3\",\n    \"audio/ogg\": \"ogg\",\n    \"video/mp4\": \"mp4\",\n    \"application/octet-stream\": \"bin\",\n  };\n  if (known[base]) return known[base];\n  const sub = base.split(\"/\")[1];\n  return sub ? sub.replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"\") || \"bin\" : \"bin\";\n}\n\n/**\n * GET a binary endpoint (media/avatars/photos) and return it either as a saved\n * file path (default — keeps the agent context small) or inline base64. Images\n * requested as base64 come back as an MCP image block the model can view.\n */\nasync function fetchBinary(path: string, mode: \"path\" | \"base64\", name: string) {\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}` },\n  });\n  const buffer = Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer());\n  if (!response.ok) {\n    throw apiError(\"GET\", path, response, buffer.toString(\"utf8\"));\n  }\n  const contentType = response.headers.get(\"content-type\") ?? \"application/octet-stream\";\n\n  if (mode === \"base64\") {\n    const data = buffer.toString(\"base64\");\n    if (contentType.split(\";\")[0]?.trim().startsWith(\"image/\")) {\n      return {\n        content: [\n          { type: \"image\" as const, data, mimeType: contentType.split(\";\")[0].trim() },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n    return asText(JSON.stringify({ contentType, bytes: buffer.length, base64: data }));\n  }\n\n  await mkdir(DOWNLOAD_DIR, { recursive: true });\n  const dest = join(DOWNLOAD_DIR, `${name}_${randomUUID()}.${extFromContentType(contentType)}`);\n  await writeFile(dest, buffer);\n  return asText(JSON.stringify({ path: dest, contentType, bytes: buffer.length }));\n}\n\n/** Percent-encode a header value the way the server's `send_media` decodes it. */\nconst encodeHeader = (value: string) => encodeURIComponent(value);\n\n/**\n * POST raw file bytes to the media endpoint: body is the bytes, metadata rides\n * in percent-encoded `x-*` headers (mirrors `src/transport/http.ts` sendMedia).\n */\nasync function sendMedia(\n  accountId: string,\n  chatId: number,\n  bytes: Buffer,\n  meta: { fileName: string; mimeType?: string; caption?: string },\n): Promise<string> {\n  const path = `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/media`;\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = {\n    Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}`,\n    \"content-type\": \"application/octet-stream\",\n    \"Idempotency-Key\": randomUUID(),\n    \"x-file-name\": encodeHeader(meta.fileName),\n  };\n  if (meta.mimeType) headers[\"x-mime-type\"] = meta.mimeType;\n  if (meta.caption !== undefined) headers[\"x-caption\"] = encodeHeader(meta.caption);\n\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    method: \"POST\",\n    headers,\n    body: bytes as unknown as BodyInit,\n  });\n  const text = await response.text();\n  if (!response.ok) throw apiError(\"POST\", path, response, text);\n  return text.length > 0 ? text : JSON.stringify({ ok: true });\n}\n\nconst server = new McpServer({ name: \"vasya-mcp\", version: \"0.1.0\" });\n\nconst accountId = z.string().describe(\"Telegram account id (from list_accounts)\");\nconst chatId = z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Chat id (bot-api format, from list_chats)\");\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_accounts\",\n  \"List the connected Telegram accounts (id, phone, connected). Account ids are required by every other tool.\",\n  {},\n  async () => asText(await api(\"GET\", \"/accounts\")),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_chats\",\n  \"List an account's chats (id, title, unread count, type user/group/channel, last message preview). Set live=true to force a fresh fetch from Telegram instead of the cache.\",\n  { accountId, live: z.boolean().optional().describe(\"Bypass the cache (slower)\") },\n  async ({ accountId, live }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats${live ? \"?source=live\" : \"\"}`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_contacts\",\n  \"List contacts: chats of type 'user' for an account.\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/contacts`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_messages\",\n  \"Read messages from a chat, newest first. Use offsetId for pagination (pass the lowest message id from the previous page). topicId reads one forum topic.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Max messages (default 50)\"),\n    offsetId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Only messages older than this id\"),\n    topicId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Forum topic id (from get_forum_topics)\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, limit, offsetId, topicId }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    if (offsetId !== undefined) params.set(\"offset_id\", String(offsetId));\n    if (topicId !== undefined) params.set(\"topic_id\", String(topicId));\n    const qs = params.size > 0 ? `?${params}` : \"\";\n    return asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages${qs}`));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"send_message\",\n  \"Send a text message to a chat (optionally into a forum topic). Returns the sent message. Rate-limited per account — on 429, wait the indicated seconds.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    text: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Message text\"),\n    topicId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Forum topic id\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, text, topicId }) =>\n    asText(\n      await api(\"POST\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages`, { text, topicId }, true),\n    ),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"forward_messages\",\n  \"Forward messages between chats. Returns the new message ids (null entries failed).\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    fromChatId: z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Source chat id\"),\n    toChatId: z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Destination chat id\"),\n    messageIds: z.array(z.coerce.number().int()).min(1).describe(\"Message ids to forward\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, fromChatId, toChatId, messageIds }) =>\n    asText(\n      await api(\n        \"POST\",\n        `/accounts/${accountId}/messages/forward`,\n        { fromChatId, toChatId, messageIds },\n        true,\n      ),\n    ),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"mark_messages_read\",\n  \"Mark messages as read in a chat up to a message id (sends the read receipt to Telegram).\",\n  { accountId, chatId, maxId: z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Highest message id to mark read\") },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, maxId }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"POST\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/read`, { maxId }, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"search_messages\",\n  \"Full-text search within one chat.\",\n  { accountId, chatId, query: z.string().min(1), limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional() },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, query, limit }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams({ q: query });\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    return asText(\n      await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages/search?${params}`),\n    );\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"search_all_messages\",\n  \"Full-text search across all chats of an account. Returns message previews with chat titles.\",\n  { accountId, query: z.string().min(1), limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional() },\n  async ({ accountId, query, limit }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams({ q: query });\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    return asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/messages/search?${params}`));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"global_search\",\n  \"Search Telegram globally for users, groups and channels by name/username (not message content).\",\n  { accountId, query: z.string().min(1), limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional() },\n  async ({ accountId, query, limit }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams({ q: query });\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    return asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/search?${params}`));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_forum_topics\",\n  \"List the topics of a forum supergroup (chats with isForum=true).\",\n  { accountId, chatId },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/topics`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_folders\",\n  \"List the account's UI folders (chat organization).\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/folders`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"start_loading_chats\",\n  \"Trigger a background refresh of the chat list from Telegram. Results land in the cache used by list_chats; poll list_chats afterwards.\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"POST\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/load`)),\n);\n\n// --- Auth / login (scope: telegram:login) -----------------------------------\n// Three-step flow: request_login_code returns an accountId, then\n// verify_login_code with the SMS/app code; if it reports password_required,\n// finish with submit_2fa_password.\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"request_login_code\",\n  \"Start a Telegram login: send a code to the phone and return a new accountId (scope: telegram:login). Continue with verify_login_code using that accountId. Requires server-side API credentials.\",\n  { phone: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Phone number in international format, e.g. +12025550123\") },\n  async ({ phone }) => asText(await api(\"POST\", \"/telegram/login/code\", { phone }, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"verify_login_code\",\n  \"Submit the login code Telegram sent. Returns {status:'authorized', user} on success or {status:'password_required'} if the account has 2FA — then call submit_2fa_password (scope: telegram:login).\",\n  {\n    accountId: z.string().describe(\"accountId from request_login_code\"),\n    code: z.string().min(1).describe(\"The login code from SMS/Telegram app\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, code }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"POST\", \"/telegram/login/verify\", { accountId, code }, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"submit_2fa_password\",\n  \"Finish a login that returned password_required by submitting the account's 2FA (cloud) password (scope: telegram:login).\",\n  {\n    accountId: z.string().describe(\"accountId from request_login_code\"),\n    password: z.string().min(1).describe(\"The 2FA cloud password\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, password }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"POST\", \"/telegram/login/password\", { accountId, password }, true)),\n);\n\n// --- Accounts ----------------------------------------------------------------\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"delete_account\",\n  \"Log out and remove a connected account from the server (scope: telegram:login). Irreversible — the session is dropped; logging back in needs the code flow again.\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"DELETE\", `/accounts/${accountId}`, undefined, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_account_avatar\",\n  \"Fetch the account owner's own profile photo (scope: accounts:read). Returns a saved file path by default, or set output='base64' to get the image inline.\",\n  { accountId, output: binaryOutput },\n  async ({ accountId, output }) =>\n    fetchBinary(`/accounts/${accountId}/avatar`, output, `avatar_${accountId}`),\n);\n\n// --- Chats -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"create_group\",\n  \"Create a basic Telegram group with the given title and initial members (scope: chats:write). Returns the new chat id (bot-api format).\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    title: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Group title\"),\n    userIds: z\n      .array(z.coerce.number().int())\n      .min(1)\n      .describe(\"User ids to add (from get_contacts/global_search)\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, title, userIds }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"POST\", `/accounts/${accountId}/groups`, { title, userIds }, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"create_channel\",\n  \"Create a broadcast channel, or a supergroup when isMegagroup=true (scope: chats:write). Returns the new chat id (bot-api format).\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    title: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Channel/supergroup title\"),\n    about: z.string().optional().describe(\"Description (optional)\"),\n    isMegagroup: z\n      .boolean()\n      .optional()\n      .describe(\"true → supergroup, false/omitted → broadcast channel\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, title, about, isMegagroup }) =>\n    asText(\n      await api(\n        \"POST\",\n        `/accounts/${accountId}/channels`,\n        { title, about, isMegagroup },\n        true,\n      ),\n    ),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"delete_chat\",\n  \"Delete/leave a chat (scope: chats:write). Channels/supergroups are left, basic groups are left, and user chats have their history cleared.\",\n  { accountId, chatId },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"DELETE\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}`, undefined, true)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_chat_photo\",\n  \"Fetch a chat/user's current profile photo (scope: chats:read). Returns a saved file path by default, or set output='base64' for the image inline.\",\n  { accountId, chatId, output: binaryOutput },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, output }) =>\n    fetchBinary(`/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/photo`, output, `chat_${chatId}`),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_user_photos\",\n  \"List all of a chat/user's profile photos (scope: chats:read). Returns {count, urls}; each url is an API path you can fetch with get_account_avatar-style auth.\",\n  { accountId, chatId },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/photos`)),\n);\n\n// --- Folders (write) ---------------------------------------------------------\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"save_folder\",\n  \"Create or update a UI folder (upsert by id; scope: folders:write). Folders organize the chat list by type and explicit include/exclude lists.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    id: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Folder id (stable key; reuse to update)\"),\n    name: z.string().min(1).describe(\"Folder display name\"),\n    icon: z.string().optional().describe(\"Optional icon name/emoji\"),\n    includedChatTypes: z\n      .array(z.string())\n      .optional()\n      .describe(\"Chat types to include, e.g. ['user','group','channel']\"),\n    excludedChatTypes: z.array(z.string()).optional().describe(\"Chat types to exclude\"),\n    includedChatIds: z.array(z.coerce.number().int()).optional().describe(\"Specific chat ids to include\"),\n    excludedChatIds: z.array(z.coerce.number().int()).optional().describe(\"Specific chat ids to exclude\"),\n    sortOrder: z.coerce.number().int().min(0).max(10000).describe(\"Sort position (0–10000)\"),\n  },\n  async ({\n    accountId,\n    id,\n    name,\n    icon,\n    includedChatTypes,\n    excludedChatTypes,\n    includedChatIds,\n    excludedChatIds,\n    sortOrder,\n  }) =>\n    asText(\n      await api(\n        \"POST\",\n        `/accounts/${accountId}/folders`,\n        {\n          // FolderRecord is snake_case on the wire (no camelCase rename).\n          id,\n          account_id: accountId,\n          name,\n          icon: icon ?? null,\n          included_chat_types: includedChatTypes ?? [2026-06-13T13:17:56.122Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "# VasyApp - Telegram Client\n\n![Vasya and the \"Vasya.app, not WhatsApp\" story](docs/assets/vasya-comic.jpg)\n\nCross-platform Telegram client built with Tauri 2.0 + React 19 + TypeScript. Features voice transcription, media handling, and optimized message loading.\n\n## 🌐 Web version\n\nThe same UI also runs in the browser against a remote **`vasya-server`** (the Telegram session host) — no Tauri runtime required. Self-hosted: bring your own server and domain.\n\n- **Connect:** open the web app, enter your vasya-server URL, then sign in with email + password (issues a JWT) or paste an access token. Builds can pre-fill the API origin via the `VITE_VASYA_API_URL` build var.\n- **Build:** `VITE_VASYA_API_URL=https://your-api.example.com npm run build` → serve `dist/`.\n- The engine transport is abstracted (`src/transport/`): Tauri IPC on desktop, HTTP/SSE in the browser. The production stack (vasya-server + sync backend + Caddy) lives in `backend/deploy/` — see its README to deploy your own.\n\n## 🤖 API-managed & agent-native\n\nThe engine (`vasya-core`) is fronted by **`vasya-server`** — an API gateway you can drive programmatically or hand to AI agents:\n\n- **REST** (`/api/v1`, ~46 routes) + **GraphQL** (queries, mutations, **WS subscriptions**) + **SSE** event stream (`/api/v1/events`) + an **OpenAPI** spec.\n- **Agent-native**: scoped API keys (`vk_…`) with a per-key permission scope, an **audit log**, and **idempotency-key** replay — safe to give an autonomous agent.\n- **MCP server** (`vasya-mcp/`): wraps the REST API as **13 Model Context Protocol tools**, so an LLM can list chats, read/send messages, manage accounts, etc. Configure with `VASYA_API_URL` + `VASYA_AGENT_KEY`.\n\n## 🧩 Deployment modes (self-hostable, no lock-in)\n\nSame UI, four ways to run the engine — pick per use case:\n\n1. **Embedded** (default desktop) — the Telegram engine runs in-process via Tauri IPC. No server, no backend.\n2. **Embedded + local API** — the desktop app mounts the API server on `127.0.0.1` (Settings → General → *Local API server*, token-auth). Lets local AI agents / MCP talk to your running app — **no separate backend needed**.\n3. **Remote / web** — the browser UI talks to a remote `vasya-server`.\n4. **Remote / desktop** — the desktop app points at a remote `vasya-server` instead of its embedded engine.\n\nRun `vasya-server` standalone or alongside the sync backend (`backend/deploy/`). Everything is self-hostable — bring your own server, domain, and secrets.\n\n## ✨ Features\n\n- **Messaging UX**: Telegram-style date separators, rich link previews, and message grouping\n\n- **Telegram Integration**: Full MTProto support via grammers-client v0.8\n- **Voice Transcription (STT)**:\n  - Cloud: Deepgram Nova-2 — bring your own API key (Settings → STT); no key is embedded in the build\n  - Local: Whisper.cpp via sidecar (~1 GB RAM)\n  - Auto-transcribe incoming voice messages\n  - Multi-language support (Russian, English, Ukrainian, German, French, Spanish)\n- **Media Handling**:\n  - Auto-download for photos/stickers/voice — viewport-scoped (only media actually on screen is fetched)\n  - Click-to-download for videos/documents (user downloads jump the queue)\n  - Media caching and queue management\n  - Paste images directly (Ctrl+V)\n- **Performance**:\n  - Message virtualization with @tanstack/react-virtual\n  - Optimistic updates with Zustand 5\n  - Connection state monitoring\n- **Multi-account**: Switch between multiple Telegram accounts\n\n## 🛠 Tech Stack\n\n**Frontend:**\n- React 19 + TypeScript\n- Zustand 5 (state management)\n- @tanstack/react-virtual (virtualization)\n- Vite (build tool)\n\n**Backend:**\n- Tauri 2.0 (Rust + system APIs)\n- grammers-client 0.8 (Telegram MTProto)\n- whisper-rs + whisper.cpp (local STT)\n- reqwest (HTTP client for Deepgram)\n\n## 🚀 Development\n\n### Prerequisites\n- Node.js 20+\n- Rust (latest stable)\n- Xcode Command Line Tools (macOS)\n\n### Setup\n```bash\n# Install dependencies\nnpm install\n\n# Create .env file (Deepgram key is NOT a build input — users add it in Settings → STT)\necho \"TELEGRAM_API_ID=your_api_id\" >> .env\necho \"TELEGRAM_API_HASH=your_api_hash\" >> .env\n\n# Run in development mode\nnpm run tauri dev\n\n# Build for production\nnpm run tauri build\n```\n\n### STT Sidecar (Optional - for local Whisper)\n```bash\ncd src-tauri/stt-sidecar\ncargo build --release\ncp target/release/stt-sidecar target/release/stt-sidecar-aarch64-apple-darwin\n```\n\n## 📱 Android Build\n\n### GitHub Actions (Recommended)\n1. Add repository secrets:\n   - `TELEGRAM_API_ID`\n   - `TELEGRAM_API_HASH`\n2. Push to `main` or trigger workflow manually\n3. Download APK from Artifacts (~15-30 minutes)\n\nSee [.github/ANDROID_BUILD.md](.github/ANDROID_BUILD.md) for details.\n\n### Local Android Build\n```bash\n# Install Android Studio + SDK + NDK r25c\nexport ANDROID_HOME=$HOME/Library/Android/sdk\nexport NDK_HOME=$ANDROID_HOME/ndk/25.0.8775105\n\n# Add Rust targets\nrustup target add aarch64-linux-android armv7-linux-androideabi\n\n# Build\nnpm run tauri android init\nnpm run tauri android build\n```\n\n## 🔐 Environment Variables\n\n| Variable | Description | Required |\n|----------|-------------|----------|\n| `TELEGRAM_API_ID` | Telegram API ID from my.telegram.org | Yes |\n| `TELEGRAM_API_HASH` | Telegram API Hash | Yes |\n\nThe Deepgram API key is a runtime user setting (Settings → STT), not a build\ninput — it is never embedded in the binary.\n\n## 📝 License\n\nMIT\n\n## 👤 Author\n\n**Евгений (suenot)**\n- GitHub: [@suenot](https://github.com/suenot)\n",

[2026-06-13T13:18:18.552Z] [INFO]           "content": "# vasya-mcp\n\nA [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io) (stdio) server that\nexposes the [vasya](../../) Telegram session host as agent tools. It is **thin by\ndesign**: every tool is one call to the REST API (`vasya-server`), so the API's\nauth, scopes, audit log and idempotency apply unchanged.\n\n## Configure\n\n| Env var | Required | Default | Purpose |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| `VASYA_AGENT_KEY` | ✅ | — | Scoped agent key (`vk_…`) or any bearer the API accepts. Create one via `POST /api/v1/agent-keys`. |\n| `VASYA_API_URL` | | `http://127.0.0.1:8787` | Base URL of a running `vasya-server`. |\n| `VASYA_DOWNLOAD_DIR` | | `<tmp>/vasya-mcp-downloads` | Where binary tools save files when `output=\"path\"`. |\n\n```jsonc\n// e.g. in an MCP client config\n{\n  \"mcpServers\": {\n    \"vasya\": {\n      \"command\": \"node\",\n      \"args\": [\"src-tauri/vasya-mcp/dist/index.js\"],\n      \"env\": { \"VASYA_API_URL\": \"http://127.0.0.1:8787\", \"VASYA_AGENT_KEY\": \"vk_...\" }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nBuild with `npm install && npm run build` (outputs `dist/index.js`).\n\n## Scopes\n\nTool availability follows the key's scopes — out-of-scope calls return the API's\n`403 Missing scope: <scope>` verbatim as the tool error, so an agent can\nself-diagnose which scope to request. Each tool below names the scope it needs.\n\n## Tools (29)\n\nEvery tool maps 1:1 to a REST route.\n\n### Accounts & login (`telegram:login`, `accounts:read`)\n| Tool | REST |\n| --- | --- |\n| `list_accounts` | `GET /accounts` |\n| `request_login_code` | `POST /telegram/login/code` |\n| `verify_login_code` | `POST /telegram/login/verify` |\n| `submit_2fa_password` | `POST /telegram/login/password` |\n| `delete_account` | `DELETE /accounts/{acc}` |\n| `get_account_avatar` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/avatar` |\n\nLogin is a flow: `request_login_code` → `verify_login_code` → (if it returns\n`password_required`) `submit_2fa_password`.\n\n### Chats (`chats:read`, `chats:write`)\n| Tool | REST |\n| --- | --- |\n| `list_chats` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/chats` |\n| `get_contacts` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/contacts` |\n| `start_loading_chats` | `POST /accounts/{acc}/chats/load` |\n| `create_group` | `POST /accounts/{acc}/groups` |\n| `create_channel` | `POST /accounts/{acc}/channels` |\n| `delete_chat` | `DELETE /accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}` |\n| `get_chat_photo` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/photo` |\n| `get_user_photos` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/photos` |\n| `get_forum_topics` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/topics` |\n\n### Messages, search & media (`messages:read`, `messages:send`)\n| Tool | REST |\n| --- | --- |\n| `get_messages` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/messages` |\n| `send_message` | `POST /accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/messages` |\n| `forward_messages` | `POST /accounts/{acc}/messages/forward` |\n| `mark_messages_read` | `POST /accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/read` |\n| `send_media` | `POST /accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/media` |\n| `download_media` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/messages/{message_id}/media` |\n| `search_messages` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/chats/{chat_id}/messages/search` |\n| `search_all_messages` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/messages/search` |\n| `global_search` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/search` |\n\n### Folders & tabs (`folders:read`, `folders:write`)\n| Tool | REST |\n| --- | --- |\n| `list_folders` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/folders` |\n| `save_folder` | `POST /accounts/{acc}/folders` |\n| `delete_folder` | `DELETE /accounts/{acc}/folders/{folder_id}` |\n| `get_tabs` | `GET /accounts/{acc}/tabs` |\n| `save_tabs` | `PUT /accounts/{acc}/tabs` |\n\n## Media notes\n\n- **`send_media`** — provide either a local `path` (read from disk) or base64\n  `data` (then `fileName` is required). Optional `caption` and `mimeType`\n  (`image/*` is sent as a photo, anything else as a document). The bytes go in\n  the raw request body; metadata rides in percent-encoded `x-file-name` /\n  `x-mime-type` / `x-caption` headers — the same contract as the desktop client.\n- **`download_media`, `get_account_avatar`, `get_chat_photo`** — return a saved\n  file path by default (`output=\"path\"`), or set `output=\"base64\"` to get the\n  bytes inline (images come back as a viewable MCP image block). Saved files go\n  to `VASYA_DOWNLOAD_DIR`.\n\n## Out of scope\n\nVoice/group calls, STT/transcription and storage-mode are still `501` on the\nREST side and have no tools yet.\n"

\"http://127.0.0.1:8787\").replace(/\\/$/, \"\");\nconst AGENT_KEY = process.env.VASYA_AGENT_KEY;\nif (!AGENT_KEY) {\n  console.error(\"VASYA_AGENT_KEY is required (create one via POST /api/v1/agent-keys)\");\n  process.exit(1);\n}\n\n// Where download_media / get_*_avatar / get_chat_photo write files when the\n// caller asks for a path (the default) instead of inline base64.\nconst DOWNLOAD_DIR = process.env.VASYA_DOWNLOAD_DIR ?? join(tmpdir(), \"vasya-mcp-downloads\");\n\nasync function api(\n  method: string,\n  path: string,\n  body?: unknown,\n  idempotent = false,\n): Promise<string> {\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = { Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}` };\n  if (body !== undefined) headers[\"content-type\"] = \"application/json\";\n  // Safe retries for mutations: the server replays on duplicate keys.\n  if (idempotent) headers[\"Idempotency-Key\"] = randomUUID();\n\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    method,\n    headers,\n    body: body === undefined ? undefined : JSON.stringify(body),\n  });\n\n  const text = await response.text();\n  if (!response.ok) {\n    const retryAfter = response.headers.get(\"retry-after\");\n    throw new Error(\n      `${method} ${path} -> ${response.status}${retryAfter ? ` (retry after ${retryAfter}s)` : \"\"}: ${text}`,\n    );\n  }\n  return text.length > 0 ? text : JSON.stringify({ ok: true });\n}\n\nconst asText = (text: string) => ({ content: [{ type: \"text\" as const, text }] });\n\n/** Build the API error message exactly like `api()` does (status + body). */\nfunction apiError(method: string, path: string, response: Response, body: string): Error {\n  const retryAfter = response.headers.get(\"retry-after\");\n  return new Error(\n    `${method} ${path} -> ${response.status}${retryAfter ? ` (retry after ${retryAfter}s)` : \"\"}: ${body}`,\n  );\n}\n\n/** Pick a file extension for a downloaded blob from its content-type. */\nfunction extFromContentType(contentType: string): string {\n  const base = contentType.split(\";\")[0]?.trim().toLowerCase() ?? \"\";\n  const known: Record<string, string> = {\n    \"image/jpeg\": \"jpg\",\n    \"image/png\": \"png\",\n    \"image/gif\": \"gif\",\n    \"image/webp\": \"webp\",\n    \"audio/mpeg\": \"mp3\",\n    \"audio/ogg\": \"ogg\",\n    \"video/mp4\": \"mp4\",\n    \"application/octet-stream\": \"bin\",\n  };\n  if (known[base]) return known[base];\n  const sub = base.split(\"/\")[1];\n  return sub ? sub.replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"\") || \"bin\" : \"bin\";\n}\n\n/**\n * GET a binary endpoint (media/avatars/photos) and return it either as a saved\n * file path (default — keeps the agent context small) or inline base64. Images\n * requested as base64 come back as an MCP image block the model can view.\n */\nasync function fetchBinary(path: string, mode: \"path\" | \"base64\", name: string) {\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}` },\n  });\n  const buffer = Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer());\n  if (!response.ok) {\n    throw apiError(\"GET\", path, response, buffer.toString(\"utf8\"));\n  }\n  const contentType = response.headers.get(\"content-type\") ?? \"application/octet-stream\";\n\n  if (mode === \"base64\") {\n    const data = buffer.toString(\"base64\");\n    if (contentType.split(\";\")[0]?.trim().startsWith(\"image/\")) {\n      return {\n        content: [\n          { type: \"image\" as const, data, mimeType: contentType.split(\";\")[0].trim() },\n        ],\n      };\n    }\n    return asText(JSON.stringify({ contentType, bytes: buffer.length, base64: data }));\n  }\n\n  await mkdir(DOWNLOAD_DIR, { recursive: true });\n  const dest = join(DOWNLOAD_DIR, `${name}_${randomUUID()}.${extFromContentType(contentType)}`);\n  await writeFile(dest, buffer);\n  return asText(JSON.stringify({ path: dest, contentType, bytes: buffer.length }));\n}\n\n/** Percent-encode a header value the way the server's `send_media` decodes it. */\nconst encodeHeader = (value: string) => encodeURIComponent(value);\n\n/**\n * POST raw file bytes to the media endpoint: body is the bytes, metadata rides\n * in percent-encoded `x-*` headers (mirrors `src/transport/http.ts` sendMedia).\n */\nasync function sendMedia(\n  accountId: string,\n  chatId: number,\n  bytes: Buffer,\n  meta: { fileName: string; mimeType?: string; caption?: string },\n): Promise<string> {\n  const path = `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/media`;\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = {\n    Authorization: `Bearer ${AGENT_KEY}`,\n    \"content-type\": \"application/octet-stream\",\n    \"Idempotency-Key\": randomUUID(),\n    \"x-file-name\": encodeHeader(meta.fileName),\n  };\n  if (meta.mimeType) headers[\"x-mime-type\"] = meta.mimeType;\n  if (meta.caption !== undefined) headers[\"x-caption\"] = encodeHeader(meta.caption);\n\n  const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}/api/v1${path}`, {\n    method: \"POST\",\n    headers,\n    body: bytes as unknown as BodyInit,\n  });\n  const text = await response.text();\n  if (!response.ok) throw apiError(\"POST\", path, response, text);\n  return text.length > 0 ? text : JSON.stringify({ ok: true });\n}\n\nconst server = new McpServer({ name: \"vasya-mcp\", version: \"0.1.0\" });\n\nconst accountId = z.string().describe(\"Telegram account id (from list_accounts)\");\nconst chatId = z.coerce.number().int().describe(\"Chat id (bot-api format, from list_chats)\");\n// How binary endpoints (avatars, photos, media) return their bytes.\nconst binaryOutput = z\n  .enum([\"path\", \"base64\"])\n  .default(\"path\")\n  .describe(\"'path' saves to disk and returns the path (default); 'base64' returns bytes inline\");\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_accounts\",\n  \"List the connected Telegram accounts (id, phone, connected). Account ids are required by every other tool.\",\n  {},\n  async () => asText(await api(\"GET\", \"/accounts\")),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"list_chats\",\n  \"List an account's chats (id, title, unread count, type user/group/channel, last message preview). Set live=true to force a fresh fetch from Telegram instead of the cache.\",\n  { accountId, live: z.boolean().optional().describe(\"Bypass the cache (slower)\") },\n  async ({ accountId, live }) =>\n    asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats${live ? \"?source=live\" : \"\"}`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_contacts\",\n  \"List contacts: chats of type 'user' for an account.\",\n  { accountId },\n  async ({ accountId }) => asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/contacts`)),\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"get_messages\",\n  \"Read messages from a chat, newest first. Use offsetId for pagination (pass the lowest message id from the previous page). topicId reads one forum topic.\",\n  {\n    accountId,\n    chatId,\n    limit: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Max messages (default 50)\"),\n    offsetId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Only messages older than this id\"),\n    topicId: z.coerce.number().int().optional().describe(\"Forum topic id (from get_forum_topics)\"),\n  },\n  async ({ accountId, chatId, limit, offsetId, topicId }) => {\n    const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    if (limit !== undefined) params.set(\"limit\", String(limit));\n    if (offsetId !== undefined) params.set(\"offset_id\", String(offsetId));\n    if (topicId !== undefined) params.set(\"topic_id\", String(topicId));\n    const qs = params.size > 0 ? `?${params}` : \"\";\n    return asText(await api(\"GET\", `/accounts/${accountId}/chats/${chatId}/messages${qs}`));\n  },\n);\n\nserver.tool(\n  \"send_message\",\n  \"Send a text message to a chat (optionally into a forum topic). Accepts either a local `path` or base64 `data`.\n- **Binary downloads** (`download_media`, `get_account_avatar`, `get_chat_photo`) return a saved file path by default (`output=\"path\"`, written to `VASYA_DOWNLOAD_DIR`) or inline base64 (`output=\"base64\"`; images come back as a viewable MCP image block).\n- **Folder/tab bodies** are sent snake_case to match `FolderRecord`/`TabRecord` (which have no camelCase rename); login/create bodies are camelCase to match their DTOs.\n- **Scope errors** pass through: the API's `403 Missing scope: <scope>` is surfaced verbatim as the tool error so the agent can self-diagnose.\n- `load_more_chats` from the issue list maps to `POST /accounts/{acc}/chats/load`, which the pre-existing `start_loading_chats` tool already covers — no duplicate tool was added.\n- Added `src-tauri/vasya-mcp/README.md` (config, scopes, full tool↔route map) and bumped the package to `0.2.0`.\n\n## Verification\n\n- ✅ `tsc --noEmit` clean in `vasya-mcp`.\n- ✅ `tools/list` returns **29** tools (13 original + 16 new).\n- ✅ Each new tool was driven over stdio against a recording mock HTTP server, confirming the exact **method + route + payload** per tool, e.g.:\n  - `create_group` → `POST /accounts/{acc}/groups` `{\"title\":...,\"userIds\":[...]}`\n  - `save_folder` → `POST /accounts/{acc}/folders` (snake_case `FolderRecord`)\n  - `save_tabs` → `PUT /accounts/{acc}/tabs` (snake_case `TabRecord[]`)\n  - `send_media` → `POST .../media` raw body + `x-file-name`/`x-caption` (percent-encoded `hi%20there`) + `Content-Type: application/octet-stream`\n  - `download_media` → `GET .../messages/{id}/media`, base64 → viewable image block\n\nFull live `tools/call` against an authenticated Telegram session (`create_group`/`save_folder`/`send_media`/`download_media`) requires real Telegram credentials + an authorized account, which aren't available in CI; the mock-server harness verifies the wiring those calls depend on.\n\n## Out of scope\nVoice/group calls, STT/transcription and storage-mode remain `501` on the REST side — no tools added (tracked separately).\nEOF\n)\" && gh pr ready 8 --repo suenot/vasya && echo \"PR updated and marked ready\"",

Here's a summary of what I did for [issue #1](https://github.com/suenot/vasya/issues/1).\n\n## What changed\n\nExpanded `vasya-mcp` (the stdio MCP server at `src-tauri/vasya-mcp/src/index.ts`) from **13 read-heavy tools to 29**, bringing it to parity with the `vasya-server` REST surface so an LLM can fully operate the messenger.\n\n**16 new tools** (each = one REST route, reusing the existing bearer/idempotency helper):\n- **Login flow:** `request_login_code`, `verify_login_code`, `submit_2fa_password`\n- **Accounts:** `delete_account`, `get_account_avatar`\n- **Chats:** `create_group`, `create_channel`, `delete_chat`, `get_chat_photo`, `get_user_photos`\n- **Folders/tabs:** `save_folder`, `delete_folder`, `get_tabs`, `save_tabs`\n- **Media:** `send_media`, `download_media`\n\nKey correctness details I verified against the route handlers:\n- `send_media` uses the raw-body + percent-encoded `x-file-name`/`x-mime-type`/`x-caption` header contract (mirrors `src/transport/http.ts`); accepts a local `path` or base64 `data`.\n- Binary endpoints (`download_media`, avatars, photos) save to `VASYA_DOWNLOAD_DIR` by default or return inline base64 (images as a viewable MCP image block).\n- Folder/tab bodies are **snake_case** (matching `FolderRecord`/`TabRecord`, which have no camelCase rename); login/create bodies are camelCase (matching their DTOs).\n- `403 Missing scope: <scope>` errors pass through verbatim.\n- `load_more_chats` from the issue list is already covered by the existing `start_loading_chats` (same `POST /chats/load` route) — no duplicate added.\n\nAlso added `src-tauri/vasya-mcp/README.md` (config, scopes, full tool↔route map), bumped the package to `0.2.0`, updated the root README's tool count, and removed the bootstrap `.gitkeep`.\n\n## Verification\n- ✅ `tsc --noEmit` clean.\n- ✅ `tools/list` returns 29 tools (13 original unchanged + 16 new).\n- ✅ Drove every new tool over stdio against a **recording mock HTTP server**, confirming exact method + route + payload for each (e.g. `create_group` → `POST /accounts/{acc}/groups {title,userIds}`; `send_media` → raw body + `x-caption: hi%20there` + `Content-Type: application/octet-stream`; `download_media` base64 → image block).\n\nLive `tools/call` against a real authenticated Telegram session isn't possible in this environment (needs real credentials + an authorized account), but the mock harness verifies all the request wiring those calls depend on.\n\nPR: https://github.com/suenot/vasya/pull/8 (updated, marked ready for review). Note: the repo's only workflows trigger on tags/releases, so there's no PR-level CI to run."

[2026-06-13T13:21:04.597Z] [STDOUT] [{"createdAt":"2026-06-13T13:12:43Z","headRefName":"issue-1-d5192f2e83f0","isDraft":false,"number":8,"state":"OPEN","title":"vasya-mcp: expand MCP tools to full REST coverage (login, media, folders, tabs, account/chat management)","updatedAt":"2026-06-13T13:21:03Z","url":"https://github.com/suenot/vasya/pull/8"}]
[2026-06-13T13:21:04.603Z] [INFO]   ✅ Found pull request #8: "vasya-mcp: expand MCP tools to full REST coverage (login, media, folders, tabs, account/chat management)"
[2026-06-13T13:21:04.933Z] [STDOUT] ## Summary

Brings `vasya-mcp` (the stdio MCP server) from **13** read-heavy tools to **29**, at parity with the `vasya-server` REST surface, so an LLM can fully operate the messenger over MCP — run the login flow, manage accounts, create/delete chats, send & download media, and write folders/tabs.

Fixes #1

## What was added (16 new tools, each = one REST route)

| Area | Tools | Scope |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Login flow | `request_login_code`, `verify_login_code`, `submit_2fa_password` | `telegram:login` |
| Accounts | `delete_account`, `get_account_avatar` | `telegram:login` / `accounts:read` |
| Chats | `create_group`, `create_channel`, `delete_chat`, `get_chat_photo`, `get_user_photos` | `chats:write` / `chats:read` |
| Folders & tabs | `save_folder`, `delete_folder`, `get_tabs`, `save_tabs` | `folders:write` / `folders:read` |
| Media | `send_media`, `download_media` | `messages:send` / `messages:read` |

The 13 original tools are **unchanged** (contracts byte-identical).

### Notes
- **Media upload** (`send_media`) follows the REST raw-body contract: bytes in the request body (`Content-Type: application/octet-stream`), metadata in percent-encoded `x-file-name` / `x-mime-type` / `x-caption` headers — mirroring `src/transport/http.ts` `sendMedia`. Accepts either a local `path` or base64 `data`.
- **Binary downloads** (`download_media`, `get_account_avatar`, `get_chat_photo`) return a saved file path by default (`output="path"`, written to `VASYA_DOWNLOAD_DIR`) or inline base64 (`output="base64"`; images come back as a viewable MCP image block).
- **Folder/tab bodies** are sent snake_case to match `FolderRecord`/`TabRecord` (which have no camelCase rename); login/create bodies are camelCase to match their DTOs.
- **Scope errors** pass through: the API's `403 Missing scope: <scope>` is surfaced verbatim as the tool error so the agent can self-diagnose.
- `load_more_chats` from the issue list maps to `POST /accounts/{acc}/chats/load`, which the pre-existing `start_loading_chats` tool already covers — no duplicate tool was added.
- Added `src-tauri/vasya-mcp/README.md` (config, scopes, full tool↔route map) and bumped the package to `0.2.0`.

## Verification

- ✅ `tsc --noEmit` clean in `vasya-mcp`.
- ✅ `tools/list` returns **29** tools (13 original + 16 new).
- ✅ Each new tool was driven over stdio against a recording mock HTTP server, confirming the exact **method + route + payload** per tool, e.g.:
  - `create_group` → `POST /accounts/{acc}/groups` `{"title":...,"userIds":[...]}`
  - `save_folder` → `POST /accounts/{acc}/folders` (snake_case `FolderRecord`)
  - `save_tabs` → `PUT /accounts/{acc}/tabs` (snake_case `TabRecord[]`)
  - `send_media` → `POST .../media` raw body + `x-file-name`/`x-caption` (percent-encoded `hi%20there`) + `Content-Type: application/octet-stream`
  - `download_media` → `GET .../messages/{id}/media`, base64 → viewable image block

Full live `tools/call` against an authenticated Telegram session (`create_group`/`save_folder`/`send_media`/`download_media`) requires real Telegram credentials + an authorized account, which aren't available in CI; the mock-server harness verifies the wiring those calls depend on.

## Out of scope
Voice/group calls, STT/transcription and storage-mode remain `501` on the REST side — no tools added (tracked separately).
